sudan.local_authority_involved: Civil Registry for death registration; Ministry of Health for documentation; active conflict involving Sudan Armed Forces (SAF) and Rapid Support Forces (RSF) makes standard procedures unreliable across most of the country
main_airports: Khartoum International Airport (KRT, currently inoperative due to conflict)
Port Sudan New International Airport (PZU, limited service)
sudan.routing_notes: Khartoum Airport has been inoperative since April 2023 due to conflict. Limited flights operate from Port Sudan to Cairo, Doha, and Jeddah. Repatriation routing requires specialist assessment at time of case — the situation is highly dynamic.
sudan.typical_cost_notes: Very wide range. Conflict conditions, airport closures, security escort requirements, and specialist firm markups make cost prediction unreliable. Cases may require body recovery from conflict-affected areas before any standard repatriation process can begin.
sudan.timeline_notes: Timelines are essentially unpredictable in current conflict conditions. Cases that can be extracted to Port Sudan or a border crossing may move in 2 to 4 months. Cases in Khartoum, Darfur, Kordofan, or other active conflict zones may take significantly longer or may not be possible to complete.
sudan.complexity_notes: Sudan is in active civil war since April 2023. The British Embassy in Khartoum suspended all operations. FCDO advises against all travel. Standard repatriation procedures are not functioning across most of the country. The Sudan Armed Forces (SAF) and the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) control different areas. Khartoum International Airport is non-operational. Only a small number of highly specialist firms with active Sudan and conflict-zone networks can manage cases. Families should not expect a standard repatriation timeline or process.
required_documents: Sudanese death certificate from Civil Registry (where accessible)
Medical certificate of cause of death (where a physician is accessible)
Any available police or military authority clearance depending on area of death
UK consular registration via FCDO emergency channels (British Embassy Khartoum suspended — consular support via FCDO London)
Certified Arabic-to-English translations of all documents
sudan.religion_notes: Sudan is approximately 97% Muslim. Islamic burial practice — swift burial, no cremation — is the norm. International repatriation from Sudan would typically involve a non-Sudanese British national or a British national of Sudanese descent returning a relative to the UK.
sudan.cremation_notes: No cremation facilities exist in Sudan. Islamic burial practice is universal. Ashes repatriation is not applicable.
no_go_zones: All of Sudan — FCDO advises against all travel
Khartoum and surrounding areas — active SAF/RSF combat
Darfur — ongoing atrocities, FCDO advises against all travel
North Kordofan, South Kordofan, Blue Nile state — active conflict
Sudanese-Eritrean border region
risk_highlights: FCDO advises against all travel to Sudan — civil war since April 2023
British Embassy Khartoum suspended operations April 2023
Khartoum Airport inoperative — only Port Sudan has limited scheduled service
SAF and RSF control different territories; authority structures vary by location
Darfur: humanitarian crisis and active atrocities — extreme risk
Even with specialist firms, repatriation from active conflict zones may not be possible

moldova.local_authority_involved: Civil Status Agency (Agenția Servicii Publice) for death registration; State Medical Examiner Service for non-natural deaths; State Inspectorate for Emergency Situations for major incidents
moldova.routing_notes: Chisinau Airport (KIV) has direct flights to London Luton (Wizz Air, FlyOne). Additional connections via Bucharest (TAROM), Vienna (Austrian), Istanbul (Turkish Airlines). Cargo follows these passenger routes. The Transnistria separatist region adds a specific regional complication.
moldova.typical_cost_notes: Moldova is an affordable repatriation destination by Eastern European standards. Costs reflect the small scale of the British national population and functioning civil infrastructure. Cases in Transnistria add significant complexity and cost.
moldova.timeline_notes: 7 to 21 days is typical for cases in Moldova proper. Cases in the Transnistria region require additional coordination and can add 2 to 4 weeks. Moldova's civil registration system functions well.
moldova.complexity_notes: Moldova is a manageable Eastern European repatriation destination with a resident British Embassy in Chisinau. The Civil Status Agency handles death registration efficiently. The main complication unique to Moldova is the Transnistria separatist region — a de facto independent territory not under Moldovan government control. Deaths in Transnistria involve Transnistrian authorities, not Moldovan ones, and the British Embassy's ability to assist is limited there. Documents are in Romanian (Moldovan); Russian is widely spoken. Direct London flights via Wizz Air.
required_documents: Moldovan death certificate issued by Civil Status Agency (Agenția Servicii Publice)
Medical certificate of cause of death
State Medical Examiner report for non-natural deaths
moldova.religion_notes: Moldova is majority Eastern Orthodox Christian (Moldovan Orthodox Church). Christian burial is the predominant practice. Cremation is available in Chisinau. A small Muslim Gagauz community exists in the south.
moldova.cremation_notes: Cremation facilities are available in Chisinau. Ashes can be repatriated to the UK as accompanied or unaccompanied baggage. Cremation documentation follows standard European procedures.
no_go_zones: Transnistria separatist region — FCDO advises against travel; British Embassy cannot provide normal consular assistance
risk_highlights: Transnistria: de facto independent separatist territory — standard Moldovan procedures do not apply
British Embassy Chisinau has limited capacity to assist in Transnistria
Romania border: some British nationals transit via Iasi (Romania) — consular support for these cases goes through British Embassy Bucharest
Direct London flights (Wizz Air to Luton) simplify cargo logistics

belarus.british_representation: British Embassy, Minsk (operating with reduced staff; FCDO advises against all but essential travel to Belarus)
belarus.local_authority_involved: Civil Registry Department (ZAGS equivalent) for death registration; Investigative Committee of Belarus for non-natural deaths; Ministry of Foreign Affairs for international document attestation
belarus.routing_notes: Direct UK-Belarus flights are suspended. Remains route via Warsaw (LOT Polish Airlines), Vilnius, or Istanbul (Turkish Airlines). The diplomatic environment since 2020 adds complexity to official documentation.
belarus.typical_cost_notes: Significant costs driven by suspended direct flights, Ministry of Foreign Affairs attestation requirements, and the political context increasing specialist firm difficulty. Cases are rare given the FCDO travel advisory and low UK tourist volume.
belarus.timeline_notes: 4 to 12 weeks is the realistic range. Belarus repatriation involves Investigative Committee clearance for non-natural deaths, Ministry of Foreign Affairs attestation for documents, and indirect routing. The political context since 2020 and further deterioration in 2022 (support for Russia's Ukraine invasion) has reduced British Embassy capacity.
belarus.complexity_notes: Belarus presents serious repatriation challenges. FCDO advises against all but essential travel. The British Embassy in Minsk operates with reduced staff following diplomatic tensions since 2020. Direct UK-Belarus flights are suspended following the forced diversion of Ryanair flight FR4978 in 2021. Belarus's Ministry of Foreign Affairs attestation process applies to all international documents. The Investigative Committee is involved in all non-natural deaths. Only specialist firms with Eastern European authority networks should handle Belarus cases.
required_documents: Belarusian death certificate from Civil Registry
Medical certificate of cause of death
Investigative Committee clearance for non-natural deaths
Ministry of Foreign Affairs attestation on all export documents
Export permit from Belarusian health authorities
belarus.religion_notes: Belarus is majority Eastern Orthodox Christian (Belarusian Orthodox Church, linked to Russian Orthodox Church). Christian burial is predominant. Cremation is available in Minsk. Catholic minority exists in western Belarus near the Polish border.
belarus.cremation_notes: Cremation facilities are available in Minsk and some larger cities. Ashes export requires cremation certificate and Ministry of Foreign Affairs attestation.
no_go_zones: Belarus-Ukraine border region — FCDO advises against all travel within 50km of Ukrainian border
All of Belarus — FCDO advises against all but essential travel
risk_highlights: FCDO advises against all but essential travel to Belarus
British Embassy Minsk reduced capacity since 2020
Direct UK-Belarus flights suspended since 2021 — indirect routing required
Ministry of Foreign Affairs attestation adds weeks to document process
Political context: Belarusian authorities have detained foreign nationals — specialist firms essential
Belarus-Ukraine border: active military logistics zone, FCDO all-travel advisory within 50km of border

tajikistan.local_authority_involved: Civil Status Registration Agency (ЗАГС) for death registration; Ministry of Internal Affairs for non-natural deaths; Ministry of Health for export documentation
tajikistan.routing_notes: Dushanbe Airport has connections to Moscow (Aeroflot, Somon Air), Istanbul (Turkish Airlines), and Dubai (flydubai). For UK repatriation, routing is typically via Istanbul or Dubai rather than Moscow (given suspended UK-Russia flights). Khujand connects mainly to Moscow. Total cargo journey 3 to 5 days once documentation cleared.
tajikistan.typical_cost_notes: Costs reflect Central Asian routing complexity, limited international cargo infrastructure, and specialist firm requirements. The mountainous terrain of Tajikistan means cases outside Dushanbe may require internal transfer first.
tajikistan.timeline_notes: 2 to 6 weeks is typical. Tajikistan has functioning civil registration but bureaucratic processes move slowly. The mountainous terrain and limited infrastructure outside Dushanbe can add to the timeline. Cases involving deaths in the Pamir region or near the Afghan border face the longest timelines.
tajikistan.complexity_notes: Tajikistan is Central Asia's poorest country and its infrastructure reflects this. There is a resident British Embassy in Dushanbe, but capacity is limited. Documents are in Tajik (Cyrillic script) and Russian. The Pamir mountains (GBAO region) are a UK adventure travel destination — deaths occur in this region among climbers and trekkers — and body recovery from high-altitude or remote locations can be a major operational challenge. The Tajik-Afghan border region has periodic security incidents. FCDO advises against all travel to GBAO (near Afghan border) and the Tajik-Kyrgyz border region.
required_documents: Tajik death certificate from Civil Status Registration Agency
Medical certificate of cause of death
Ministry of Internal Affairs clearance for non-natural deaths
tajikistan.religion_notes: Tajikistan is approximately 95% Muslim (predominantly Sunni; Ismaili Muslims in the Pamir/GBAO region). Islamic burial practice is the norm — swift burial, no cremation. International repatriation from Tajikistan typically involves an adventure traveller, aid worker, or a member of the UK Tajik diaspora.
tajikistan.cremation_notes: No cremation facilities exist in Tajikistan. Islamic burial practice is universal. Body repatriation is the only option.
no_go_zones: GBAO (Gorno-Badakhshan Autonomous Oblast) bordering Afghanistan — FCDO advises against all travel
Tajik-Afghan border region
Tajik-Kyrgyz border region (disputed areas) — FCDO advises against all travel
risk_highlights: GBAO and Pamir region: FCDO all-travel advisory — adventure tourism deaths occur here
High-altitude body recovery in Pamirs may require helicopter or specialist mountain rescue
Documents in Tajik Cyrillic and Russian — certified translation required
Limited Embassy capacity in Dushanbe
Cases near Afghan border involve additional security and authority clearances

guyana.local_authority_involved: General Register Office for death registration; Magistrate's Court for coroner inquests; police for non-natural deaths; Ministry of Public Health for export documentation
main_airports: Eugene F. Correia International Airport (OGL), Ogle (domestic and regional)
guyana.routing_notes: Guyana has no direct UK flights. Repatriation routes via Miami (American Airlines, Caribbean Airlines), New York JFK (Caribbean Airlines), or Barbados/Trinidad as Caribbean hub connections. Caribbean Airlines flies Georgetown-London Gatwick with connection. Total cargo journey 3 to 6 days from Georgetown to the UK.
guyana.typical_cost_notes: Costs reflect Caribbean routing via Miami or Trinidad. Guyana is an English-speaking Commonwealth country, which simplifies documentation. The interior jungle regions add significant cost for body recovery and transfer to Georgetown.
guyana.timeline_notes: 10 to 28 days is typical. Guyana's General Register Office processes death certificates in reasonable time. For deaths in the interior — jungle mining camps, the Rupununi savanna — internal transfer to Georgetown is the primary time variable. Post-mortem examination in Georgetown is required for all non-natural deaths.
guyana.complexity_notes: Guyana is a Commonwealth country with English as the official language, which significantly simplifies documentation compared to most South American repatriations. The British High Commission in Georgetown is resident. The main complexity is geography: much of Guyana is dense Amazonian jungle interior accessible only by small aircraft or river boat. Deaths at interior mining camps (gold and diamond mining is extensive), eco-tourism lodges, or wilderness areas require specialist recovery before standard repatriation can proceed. The growing oil and gas industry (offshore Atlantic) brings more international workers and a higher risk profile.
required_documents: Guyanese death certificate from General Register Office
Medical certificate of cause of death
Post-mortem report for non-natural deaths (Georgetown Public Hospital pathologist)
Police report for violent or suspicious deaths
Ministry of Public Health export permit
guyana.religion_notes: Guyana has significant Christian (Protestant and Catholic), Hindu, and Muslim populations, reflecting its Indo-Guyanese, Afro-Guyanese, and Amerindian communities. Christian and Hindu communities generally accept repatriation; Muslim families may prefer swift local burial. Cremation is accepted within the Hindu community.
guyana.cremation_notes: Cremation facilities are available in Georgetown. The Hindu community uses cremation. Ashes can be repatriated to the UK. Export of ashes requires cremation certificate and Ministry of Public Health clearance.
risk_highlights: Interior jungle regions: body recovery requires small aircraft or river boat — adds days to process
Gold and diamond mining interior: occupational deaths in remote camp locations
Georgetown: petty crime and road traffic incidents are most common causes of UK tourist deaths
Offshore oil and gas industry: maritime deaths require specialist recovery procedures
Guyana-Venezuela border region: FCDO advises against travel to border zone

brunei.british_representation: British High Commission Bandar Seri Begawan
brunei.local_authority_involved: Royal Brunei Police Force; Attorney General's Chambers; Department of Religious Affairs (for Muslim deaths)
brunei.routing_notes: Direct flights London Heathrow to Bandar Seri Begawan (Royal Brunei Airlines RB001/RB002). Standard cargo on Royal Brunei Airlines or via Singapore SIN.
brunei.typical_cost_notes: Standard repatriation, no major complications expected.
brunei.timeline_notes: Standard civil cases 7-14 days. Muslim deaths must follow Islamic funeral law process via Department of Religious Affairs before export clearance, which may add 2-5 days.
brunei.complexity_notes: Moderate complexity. Islam is the state religion: Muslim deaths follow Sharia-based Religious Affairs process before civil export clearance. Non-Muslim expatriate deaths follow civil law only. British Garrison Brunei provides a specialist military repatriation track for service deaths.
required_documents: Department of Religious Affairs clearance (Muslim deaths)
brunei.religion_notes: Islam is the state religion. Muslim deaths require Department of Religious Affairs clearance before export. Non-Muslim deaths follow civil law. British High Commission advises on specific religious authority requirements.
brunei.cremation_notes: Cremation is not available for Muslim deaths by law. Cremation technically possible for non-Muslims but extremely uncommon in Brunei.
risk_highlights: Sharia law applies to Muslim deaths — Religious Affairs clearance required before export
British Garrison Brunei provides specialist military repatriation track for service deaths
Direct Royal Brunei Airlines London Heathrow to BWN simplifies cargo routing

democratic-republic-of-congo.local_authority_involved: Parquet (state prosecutor); Police Nationale Congolaise; Institut National Medico-Legal (INML)
democratic-republic-of-congo.routing_notes: Kinshasa via Brussels (Brussels Airlines), Addis Ababa (Ethiopian Airlines), Nairobi (Kenya Airways). Eastern DRC (Goma) may route via Nairobi or Kigali.
democratic-republic-of-congo.typical_cost_notes: Wide range reflecting significant variation by location — Kinshasa accessible vs. eastern provinces highly complex or inaccessible.
democratic-republic-of-congo.timeline_notes: Kinshasa standard cases 21-42 days. Eastern DRC conflict areas (North Kivu, South Kivu, Ituri) significantly longer where accessible at all. Bodies in active combat areas may not be recoverable.
democratic-republic-of-congo.complexity_notes: Very high complexity. Ongoing armed conflict in eastern provinces. Bureaucratic layers in Kinshasa. INML capacity is limited. FCDO advises against all travel to eastern DRC provinces. Repatriation from Kinshasa is achievable; from conflict zones it may not be.
democratic-republic-of-congo.religion_notes: Predominantly Christian (Catholic, Protestant, Kimbanguist). Local funeral practices vary significantly by region and community.
democratic-republic-of-congo.cremation_notes: No commercial cremation facilities in DRC.
no_go_zones: North Kivu
South Kivu
Ituri province
Tanganyika province conflict areas
Areas near DRC-Rwanda border
Areas near DRC-Uganda border
risk_highlights: Ongoing armed conflict in eastern DRC — repatriation may not be achievable from conflict zones
FCDO advises against all travel to North Kivu, South Kivu, and Ituri
INML forensic capacity in Kinshasa is limited — delays common
Bodies in eastern conflict areas may be inaccessible

eritrea.local_authority_involved: Ministry of Health; Regional Administration; National Security (PFDJ)
eritrea.routing_notes: Asmara via Cairo (EgyptAir), Dubai (connections via Ethiopian Airlines or flydubai), Addis Ababa (Ethiopian Airlines). Limited direct international service.
eritrea.typical_cost_notes: High costs reflect limited airline access, specialist firm requirement, and unpredictable timelines.
eritrea.timeline_notes: 28-90 days. The government controls all administrative processes. Delays occur without explanation. Families should plan for the maximum end of this range.
eritrea.complexity_notes: Very high complexity. Eritrea operates as one of the world's most closed states. Government (PFDJ) controls all administrative outcomes. British Embassy Asmara has limited capacity to intervene in domestic processes. UK-Eritrean diaspora repatriations are the primary use case.
eritrea.religion_notes: Predominantly Eritrean Orthodox Christian and Sunni Muslim. Both religious communities have established burial practices. Government controls all religious institutions.
eritrea.cremation_notes: No cremation in Eritrea. Orthodox Christian and Islamic burial practices predominant.
no_go_zones: Eritrean-Ethiopian border areas
Eritrean-Djibouti border areas
Eritrean-Sudanese border areas
risk_highlights: One of the world's most closed states — government controls all administrative outcomes
National security clearance may be required without prior notice
British Embassy Asmara has limited ability to intervene in domestic processes
UK-Eritrean diaspora is the primary use case for this repatriation route

mongolia.local_authority_involved: National Police Agency; State Forensic Science Center; Civil Registration and Information Department
mongolia.routing_notes: Ulaanbaatar via Beijing (MIAT Mongolian Airlines, Air China), Seoul (Korean Air, MIAT). No direct UK-Mongolia service. Beijing or Seoul are the primary cargo transit points.
mongolia.typical_cost_notes: Remote interior recovery adds significant cost above urban Ulaanbaatar cases.
mongolia.timeline_notes: 14-35 days. State Forensic Science Center required for non-natural deaths. Remote steppe, Gobi desert, or Altai mountain deaths add body recovery time before standard process begins.
mongolia.complexity_notes: Moderate complexity. State Forensic Science Center handles all non-natural deaths. Remote death locations (Gobi desert, steppe, Altai mountains) require helicopter or ground recovery — a UK adventure tourism factor. British Embassy Ulaanbaatar is resident and experienced with UK traveller deaths.
required_documents: State Forensic Science Center certificate (non-natural deaths)
mongolia.religion_notes: Predominantly Tibetan Buddhist and shamanist. No religious restrictions on repatriation. Urban Ulaanbaatar cases largely follow civil process.
mongolia.cremation_notes: Cremation available in Ulaanbaatar. No cremation facilities outside urban centres.
risk_highlights: Remote deaths (Gobi desert, steppe, Altai) require specialist body recovery before standard process
Adventure tourism and horse trekking a growing UK traveller segment with associated death risk
New Chinggis Khaan International Airport opened 2021 — cargo routing updated from old Buyant-Ukhaa airport

eswatini.local_authority_involved: Royal Eswatini Police Service; Ministry of Health; Registrar General
eswatini.routing_notes: No direct UK-Eswatini service. All routing via Johannesburg O.R. Tambo International (JNB) with multiple daily connections to London Heathrow. Alternatively via Nairobi NBO or Addis Ababa ADD.
eswatini.typical_cost_notes: Standard Southern Africa routing via Johannesburg. English-language process reduces administrative cost.
eswatini.timeline_notes: 10-21 days for standard cases. English-language documentation throughout. JNB transit adds 1-2 days compared to direct-flight destinations.
eswatini.complexity_notes: Moderate complexity. English is an official language — documentation accessible. British High Commission Mbabane resident. Absolute monarchy: all administrative decisions are ultimately centralised through royal government structures, which creates predictability in process but limited room to challenge delays.
eswatini.religion_notes: Mixed Christian (Zionist, Catholic, Protestant) and Swazi traditional religion. No religious restrictions on repatriation.
eswatini.cremation_notes: No commercial cremation facilities in Eswatini. All repatriations involve body transport.
risk_highlights: All routing via Johannesburg JNB — cargo specialist with JNB experience essential
Absolute monarchy centralises all administrative decisions
English-language documentation throughout simplifies process significantly

djibouti.local_authority_involved: Police Nationale; Ministry of Health; Tribunal de Première Instance (for non-natural deaths)
djibouti.routing_notes: Djibouti via Addis Ababa (Ethiopian Airlines), Dubai (flydubai, Air Arabia), Istanbul (Turkish Airlines). Direct Addis Ababa route is primary for UK cargo connections.
djibouti.typical_cost_notes: Non-resident Embassy adds consular coordination costs. French-language documentation requires certified translation throughout.
djibouti.timeline_notes: 14-42 days. No resident British Embassy — all consular support via Addis Ababa. French/Arabic documentation requires certified translation. Tribunal involvement for non-natural deaths adds time.
djibouti.complexity_notes: Moderate complexity. No resident British Embassy in country — covered non-residently from Addis Ababa, Ethiopia. French-language civil law system. Strategic location (French and US military bases) does not affect civilian repatriation. Transit routing via Addis Ababa is well-established for UK cargo.
required_documents: Tribunal de Première Instance clearance (non-natural deaths)
djibouti.religion_notes: Islam is the state religion (Sunni). Somali and Afar communities have established Islamic burial practices. French civil law applies to civil registration regardless of religion.
djibouti.cremation_notes: No cremation in Djibouti. Predominantly Muslim country.
no_go_zones: Areas near Djibouti-Eritrea border
Remote interior near Eritrean border (disputed territory)
risk_highlights: No resident British Embassy — all consular support via non-resident coverage from Addis Ababa
French-language civil law system — all documentation in French requires certified translation
Tribunal de Première Instance involvement for non-natural deaths — judicial process adds time

lesotho.local_authority_involved: Lesotho Mounted Police Service; Ministry of Health; Registrar of Births and Deaths
lesotho.routing_notes: No direct international flights beyond Johannesburg. All routing via Johannesburg O.R. Tambo International (JNB). Maseru to JNB: South African Airlink or road transfer (~5 hours).
lesotho.typical_cost_notes: English documentation and JNB routing keep costs manageable. Mountain terrain deaths may add recovery cost.
lesotho.timeline_notes: 10-21 days. English documentation throughout. All cargo via JNB. Mountain deaths in Maluti or Drakensberg ranges may require ground recovery before standard process.
lesotho.complexity_notes: Moderate complexity. English is official language — documentation accessible. British High Commission resident in Maseru. Lesotho is a constitutional monarchy completely surrounded by South Africa. All cargo must transit Johannesburg. Highlanders (mountain region) deaths require ground recovery — Lesotho has very limited aviation outside Maseru.
lesotho.religion_notes: Predominantly Christian (Catholic and Evangelical). No religious restrictions on repatriation.
lesotho.cremation_notes: No cremation facilities in Lesotho. All repatriations involve body transport.
risk_highlights: All cargo must route via Johannesburg — no direct international air service
Mountain deaths (Maluti/Drakensberg highland areas) require ground recovery before standard process
British High Commission Maseru is resident and experienced

suriname.british_representation: British Embassy Georgetown, Guyana (non-resident accreditation for Suriname)
suriname.local_authority_involved: Korps Politie Suriname (KPS); Bureau voor Openbare Gezondheidszorg (BOG); Civil Registry (Centraal Bureau Burgerzaken)

suriname.routing_notes: Paramaribo via Miami (Surinam Airways, Caribbean Airlines), Amsterdam (KLM, TUI fly Netherlands), Port of Spain Trinidad (Caribbean Airlines). Amsterdam route most relevant for UK cargo.
suriname.typical_cost_notes: Dutch-language documentation requires certified translation throughout. Non-resident Embassy adds coordination cost.
suriname.timeline_notes: 14-35 days. Dutch civil law system — all documentation in Dutch. No resident British Embassy — covered from Georgetown, Guyana. Interior jungle deaths add recovery time.
suriname.complexity_notes: Moderate complexity. Dutch-speaking country on the South American coast, bordered by Guyana, Brazil, and French Guiana. No resident British Embassy — non-resident coverage from Georgetown. Dutch civil law framework. Interior rainforest deaths (gold mining areas, eco-tourism) require river or air recovery. Amsterdam KLM route is standard for UK cargo.
suriname.religion_notes: Highly diverse: Hindu, Muslim, Christian (various), and Winti (Surinamese Afro-Caribbean religion). No single dominant religious framework for burial — family practice varies.
suriname.cremation_notes: Cremation available in Paramaribo. Hindu community uses cremation regularly. Ashes can be transported to the UK as cremated remains.
no_go_zones: Interior gold mining areas (informal settlements — Maroon and Amerindian interior territory)
risk_highlights: Dutch-language civil law — all documents require certified translation
No resident British Embassy — non-resident coverage from Georgetown, Guyana
Interior gold mining area deaths (French Guiana border region) require river or air recovery
Amsterdam KLM route is the most reliable UK cargo pathway

timor-leste.british_representation: Australian Embassy Dili (consular assistance for British nationals under UK-Australia sharing arrangement)

timor-leste.routing_notes: Dili via Darwin, Australia (Airnorth); Bali, Indonesia (TransNusa, Garuda); Singapore (Silk Air/Scoot). UK routing via Bali–Singapore–London or Darwin–Sydney–London.
timor-leste.typical_cost_notes: Multi-leg routing via Indonesia or Australia adds cargo time and cost. Portuguese/Tetum documentation requires certified translation.
timor-leste.timeline_notes: 14-42 days. No resident British Embassy — Australian Embassy provides consular assistance. Portuguese-language civil law (Portuguese colonial system). Limited mortuary infrastructure outside Dili.
timor-leste.complexity_notes: Moderate complexity. Timor-Leste became independent in 2002 — civil registration systems are still maturing. No resident British Embassy: British nationals receive consular assistance from the Australian Embassy under the UK-Australia sharing arrangement. Portuguese civil law framework. Very limited mortuary facilities outside Dili. Routing via Bali or Darwin for UK cargo.
timor-leste.religion_notes: Predominantly Roman Catholic (over 96%). No religious restrictions on repatriation.
timor-leste.cremation_notes: No cremation facilities in Timor-Leste. All repatriations involve body transport.
no_go_zones: Remote border areas with Indonesia (West Timor)
risk_highlights: No resident British Embassy — Australian Embassy provides consular assistance under UK-Australia sharing
Portuguese-language civil law system — all documentation requires certified translation
Limited mortuary infrastructure outside capital Dili
Multi-leg routing via Bali or Darwin adds 1-3 days to cargo journey

niger.british_representation: British Embassy Abuja, Nigeria (non-resident accreditation for Niger)
niger.local_authority_involved: Police Nationale du Niger; Ministry of Public Health; Civil Status Office
niger.routing_notes: Niamey via Addis Ababa (Ethiopian Airlines), Casablanca (Royal Air Maroc), Paris CDG (Air France, when operating). Air France and Ethiopian are the primary routes for UK cargo connections.
niger.typical_cost_notes: FCDO against all but essential travel. Military junta since July 2023 has reduced international airline access. Non-resident Embassy. High specialist firm requirement.
niger.timeline_notes: 28-90 days post-coup. Military junta (CNSP) controls administrative outcomes. ECOWAS sanctions period (now partially lifted) disrupted access. French civil law documentation. Non-resident British Embassy.
niger.complexity_notes: Very high complexity. Military coup July 2023 (CNSP). FCDO advises against all but essential travel. No resident British Embassy — non-resident coverage from Abuja. French civil law documentation. Air France suspended Niamey service after the coup (service status variable). Limited international flights to Niamey. UK deaths in Niger are rare and primarily involve aid/development workers and journalists.
required_documents: Ministry of Public Health export permit
niger.religion_notes: Predominantly Sunni Muslim (over 98%). Islamic burial practice is standard. French civil law applies to civil registration.
niger.cremation_notes: No cremation in Niger. Islamic burial practices predominant.

FCDO advises against all but essential travel to Niger

mali.complexity_notes: Military junta (Transition government under Col. Goïta) controls all administrative processes since 2021. FCDO advises against all travel to northern Mali and all but essential to most of the country. JNIM and ISWAP-affiliated armed groups active across large areas.
mali.religion_notes: Predominantly Muslim. Islamic burial customs apply — rapid local interment traditional and expected.

somalia.british_representation: British Embassy Mogadishu (under severe security restrictions)

somalia.routing_notes: Turkish Airlines via Istanbul (IST) — most reliable UK route from Mogadishu. Ethiopian Airlines via Addis Ababa (ADD). Hargeisa/Somaliland: Ethiopian Airlines via Addis Ababa.

mauritania.religion_notes: Almost entirely Sunni Muslim. Strong Islamic burial pressure — rapid interment expected.
mauritania.cremation_notes: Cremation not available. Contrary to Islamic law.
no_go_zones: Saharan interior (risk of kidnap — FCDO against all travel)
Eastern border areas near Mali
risk_highlights: Non-resident Embassy — coordination via Rabat
FCDO against all travel to Saharan interior
Islamic burial pressure for rapid interment
Arabic-language documentation — translation required

central-african-republic.local_authority_involved: Forces Armées Centrafricaines (FACA), Ministry of Health, civil registrar
central-african-republic.routing_notes: Ethiopian Airlines via Addis Ababa (ADD); Air France via Paris CDG (BGF service); Kenya Airways via Nairobi. Ethiopian Airlines most reliable UK cargo route.
central-african-republic.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
central-african-republic.timeline_notes: FCDO advises against all but essential travel to Bangui and against all travel to the rest of the country. Armed groups (including ex-Séléka and anti-Balaka factions) control large parts of the country. Cases outside Bangui are not achievable.
central-african-republic.complexity_notes: CAR is consistently ranked among the world's most fragile states. Russian Wagner Group (Africa Corps) has significant influence. MINUSCA UN peacekeeping force present. Armed groups control most of the country outside Bangui. Non-resident Embassy via Yaoundé. FCDO against all travel except Bangui (all-but-essential).
no_go_zones: All areas outside Bangui (FCDO against all travel)
Border regions with Sudan, South Sudan, DRC, Cameroon, Congo
risk_highlights: FCDO against all travel outside Bangui
Armed groups control most of the country
Non-resident Embassy — coordination via Yaoundé
Wagner/Africa Corps influence on government operations
UN MINUSCA present but country remains severely unstable

guinea-bissau.british_representation: Non-resident. UK consular services provided by the British Embassy in Dakar, Senegal.
guinea-bissau.local_authority_involved: Conservatória do Registo Civil (Civil Registry), Polícia Judiciária
guinea-bissau.routing_notes: Most repatriations route via Dakar (Senegal) or Lisbon (Portugal) using TAP Air Portugal or Air Senegal. Dakar is the primary regional hub.
guinea-bissau.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
guinea-bissau.timeline_notes: Portuguese civil law documentation through the Conservatória do Registo Civil. Export permit issued by the Ministério da Administração Territorial. Non-resident embassy coverage from Dakar adds coordination time.
guinea-bissau.complexity_notes: Portuguese-language documentation in a country without resident UK diplomatic presence. Institutional capacity is limited outside Bissau. Drug trafficking routes and a history of political coups create an unpredictable environment. FCDO advises heightened vigilance in border areas with Guinea and Senegal's Casamance region.
required_documents: Consular death registration with British Embassy Dakar
guinea-bissau.religion_notes: Mix of Islam (c.45%), indigenous beliefs (c.31%), Christianity (c.22%). Islamic burial timeline pressure may apply. Allow for local traditional funerary customs.
guinea-bissau.cremation_notes: Cremation facilities are not available in Guinea-Bissau. Remains must be repatriated for cremation in the UK.
no_go_zones: Northern border regions near Senegal's Casamance area
Remote eastern border areas with Guinea
risk_highlights: Political instability and history of coups
Limited infrastructure outside Bissau
Drug trafficking routes through the country
Non-resident UK embassy adds coordination delays

benin.british_representation: Non-resident. UK consular services provided by the British High Commission in Accra, Ghana.
benin.local_authority_involved: Tribunal de Première Instance, Service de l'État Civil
benin.routing_notes: Repatriations typically route via Paris (Air France) or Casablanca (Royal Air Maroc). Ethiopian Airlines serves Cotonou via Addis Ababa.
benin.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
benin.timeline_notes: French civil law documentation through Tribunal de Première Instance. Parquet authorisation required before export. Non-resident British High Commission Accra handles consular death registration.
benin.complexity_notes: Functioning civil registration in Cotonou and major towns. Main complexity is non-resident UK coverage and French-language documentation requirements. Northern border areas near Burkina Faso and Niger have elevated terrorist threat. FCDO advises against all travel within 5km of the Burkina Faso border.
required_documents: Consular death registration with British High Commission Accra
benin.religion_notes: Mix of Christianity (c.48%), Vodoun and indigenous beliefs (c.28%), Islam (c.24%). Vodoun traditions may involve specific funerary customs that affect timing.
benin.cremation_notes: Cremation facilities are not available in Benin. Remains must be repatriated for cremation in the UK.
no_go_zones: Alibori and Atacora departments (northern border with Burkina Faso)
Border areas with Niger (Malanville area)
risk_highlights: FCDO advises against all travel within 5km of Burkina Faso border
Jihadist spillover from Sahel into northern departments
Non-resident UK coverage from Accra adds coordination time

togo.british_representation: Non-resident. UK consular services provided by the British High Commission in Accra, Ghana.
togo.local_authority_involved: Tribunal de Première Instance, Service de l'État Civil
togo.routing_notes: Repatriations typically route via Paris (Air France) or Casablanca (Royal Air Maroc). Brussels Airlines and Ethiopian Airlines also serve Lomé.
togo.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
togo.timeline_notes: French civil law documentation through Tribunal de Première Instance. Parquet authorisation required before export. Non-resident British High Commission Accra handles consular death registration.
togo.complexity_notes: Civil registration functions in Lomé and larger towns. Rural documentation gaps can extend timelines. Non-resident UK embassy coverage from Accra. Savanes region in the north has elevated risk due to jihadist spillover from Burkina Faso.
required_documents: Consular death registration with British High Commission Accra
togo.religion_notes: Mix of Christianity (c.43%), indigenous beliefs (c.35%), Islam (c.14%). Traditional funerary practices vary significantly by ethnic group and region.
togo.cremation_notes: Cremation facilities are not available in Togo. Remains must be repatriated for cremation in the UK.
no_go_zones: Savanes region (northern Togo, border with Burkina Faso)
risk_highlights: FCDO advises against all but essential travel to the Savanes region
Jihadist spillover from Sahel into far north
Non-resident UK coverage from Accra adds coordination time

gabon.british_representation: Non-resident. UK consular services provided by the British High Commission in Yaoundé, Cameroon.
gabon.local_authority_involved: Tribunal de Première Instance, Service de l'État Civil
gabon.routing_notes: Repatriations route via Paris (Air France operates Libreville to Paris CDG) or via Casablanca (Royal Air Maroc). Ethiopian Airlines serves Libreville via Addis Ababa.
gabon.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
gabon.timeline_notes: French civil law documentation through Tribunal de Première Instance. Military transition government since August 2023 may affect administrative processing speeds. Non-resident British High Commission Yaoundé handles consular registration.
gabon.complexity_notes: The Comité de Transition et de Restauration des Institutions (CTRI) has governed since the August 30 2023 coup ousting President Ali Bongo. Civil administration continues to function but bureaucratic unpredictability remains during transition. Dense forest interior and remote provinces present logistical challenges. Non-resident UK embassy adds coordination time.
required_documents: Consular death registration with British High Commission Yaoundé
gabon.religion_notes: Predominantly Christian (c.75%), with Islam (c.12%) and indigenous beliefs. No significant Islamic burial timeline pressure in most cases.
gabon.cremation_notes: Cremation facilities are not available in Gabon. Remains must be repatriated for cremation in the UK.
no_go_zones: Remote border areas with Cameroon and Republic of Congo
risk_highlights: Military coup August 2023 — CTRI transitional government in place
Administrative unpredictability during political transition
Remote equatorial forest regions with limited infrastructure and no road access
Non-resident UK coverage from Yaoundé adds coordination time

republic-of-congo.british_representation: Non-resident. UK consular services provided by the British Embassy in Kinshasa, Democratic Republic of Congo.
republic-of-congo.local_authority_involved: Tribunal de Grande Instance, Service de l'État Civil
republic-of-congo.routing_notes: Repatriations from Brazzaville route via Paris (Air France) or Addis Ababa (Ethiopian Airlines). Pointe-Noire has direct Air France service. Turkish Airlines serves Brazzaville via Istanbul.
republic-of-congo.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
republic-of-congo.timeline_notes: French civil law documentation through Tribunal de Grande Instance. Civil registration infrastructure is stronger in Brazzaville and Pointe-Noire than in rural areas. Non-resident British Embassy Kinshasa handles consular registration.
republic-of-congo.complexity_notes: Pool department south of Brazzaville has a history of armed militia activity. Rural northern jungle regions (Likouala, Sangha) have very limited infrastructure and logistical access. FCDO advises against all but essential travel to the Pool department. Non-resident UK embassy in Kinshasa across the Congo River adds coordination time.
required_documents: Consular death registration with British Embassy Kinshasa
republic-of-congo.religion_notes: Predominantly Christian (c.85%), with a small Muslim minority. No significant Islamic burial timeline pressure in most cases.
republic-of-congo.cremation_notes: Cremation facilities are not available in the Republic of Congo. Remains must be repatriated for cremation in the UK.
no_go_zones: Pool department (history of armed militia activity)
Remote northern jungle regions (Likouala, Sangha departments)
risk_highlights: FCDO advises against all but essential travel to the Pool department
Armed militia history in Pool region south of Brazzaville
Very limited infrastructure in remote northern jungle provinces
Non-resident UK coverage from Kinshasa adds coordination time

sao-tome-and-principe.british_representation: Non-resident. UK consular services provided by the British High Commission in Yaoundé, Cameroon.
sao-tome-and-principe.local_authority_involved: Conservatória do Registo Civil, Ministério do Interior
sao-tome-and-principe.routing_notes: Repatriations route via Lisbon (TAP Air Portugal operates São Tomé to Lisbon) or via Luanda (TAAG Angola Airlines). The Lisbon connection is the standard repatriation route to the UK.
sao-tome-and-principe.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
sao-tome-and-principe.timeline_notes: Portuguese civil law documentation through the Conservatória do Registo Civil. Export permit issued by the Ministério do Interior. Non-resident British High Commission Yaoundé adds coordination time. Island geography means all transport is via São Tomé International Airport.
sao-tome-and-principe.complexity_notes: Portuguese-language documentation, non-resident UK diplomatic presence, and island geography with limited direct flights create a challenging repatriation environment. Príncipe island has additional logistical layers — internal transfer to São Tomé is required before international repatriation can begin. Cold storage and mortuary capacity on the islands is limited.
required_documents: Consular death registration with British High Commission Yaoundé
sao-tome-and-principe.religion_notes: Predominantly Catholic (c.85%), with Protestant and indigenous traditions. No significant Islamic burial timeline pressure.
sao-tome-and-principe.cremation_notes: Cremation facilities are not available in São Tomé and Príncipe. Remains must be repatriated to the UK for cremation.
risk_highlights: Island geography — all repatriation exits via São Tomé International Airport
Deaths on Príncipe island require internal transfer first
Limited mortuary and cold storage capacity
Non-resident UK coverage from Yaoundé adds coordination time

equatorial-guinea.british_representation: Non-resident. UK consular services provided by the British High Commission in Yaoundé, Cameroon.
equatorial-guinea.local_authority_involved: Registro Civil, Ministerio del Interior y Corporaciones Locales
equatorial-guinea.routing_notes: Malabo (Bioko Island) is the main international gateway. Air France operates Malabo to Paris CDG. Ethiopian Airlines serves Malabo via Addis Ababa. The mainland Río Muni region uses Malabo as the hub airport after internal transfer.
equatorial-guinea.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
equatorial-guinea.timeline_notes: Spanish civil law documentation through the Registro Civil. Export authorisation from the Ministerio del Interior. Non-resident British High Commission Yaoundé adds coordination time. Deaths on the mainland (Río Muni) require transfer to Malabo before international repatriation.
equatorial-guinea.complexity_notes: Equatorial Guinea has the only Spanish-speaking government in sub-Saharan Africa. The country is split between Bioko Island (Malabo) and the mainland Río Muni enclave. Authoritarian governance under President Teodoro Obiang Nguema since 1979 means bureaucratic processes can be unpredictable. Non-resident UK coverage from Yaoundé adds coordination complexity. Freedom of movement for foreign nationals is restricted in some areas.
required_documents: Consular death registration with British High Commission Yaoundé
equatorial-guinea.religion_notes: Predominantly Catholic (c.88%), with a small Protestant minority. No significant Islamic burial timeline pressure.
equatorial-guinea.cremation_notes: Cremation facilities are not available in Equatorial Guinea. Remains must be repatriated to the UK for cremation.
no_go_zones: Border areas between Río Muni and Cameroon/Gabon
risk_highlights: Authoritarian governance — bureaucratic unpredictability
Country split between island (Bioko) and mainland (Río Muni) — internal transfer required for mainland deaths
Non-resident UK coverage from Yaoundé adds coordination time
Restrictions on freedom of movement for foreign nationals

haiti.british_representation: Non-resident. UK consular services provided by the British High Commission in Kingston, Jamaica.
haiti.routing_notes: Repatriations route via Miami (American Airlines), New York (JFK), or via other Caribbean hubs. Air France previously operated direct Paris service; connectivity is limited and subject to security-driven schedule changes.
haiti.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
haiti.timeline_notes: Repatriation from Haiti is severely complicated by gang violence, a dysfunctional state apparatus, and ongoing political instability. Civil registration has collapsed in large parts of Port-au-Prince. Timelines are highly unpredictable and heavily dependent on security conditions at the time of death.
haiti.complexity_notes: FCDO advises against all but essential travel to Haiti. Gang violence controls large parts of Port-au-Prince and other cities. The Multinational Security Support Mission (MSS), led by Kenya, began deploying in 2024 but the security situation remains severe. Civil registration infrastructure has collapsed in gang-controlled areas. The Office National d'Identification (ONI) processes death documentation but access to offices in insecure areas is not guaranteed. Cold storage and mortuary capacity is extremely limited outside the capital.
required_documents: Consular death registration with British High Commission Kingston
haiti.religion_notes: Mix of Catholicism, Protestantism, and Vodou traditions. Vodou funerary practices vary widely and may affect timing and handling of remains.
haiti.cremation_notes: Cremation facilities are not available in Haiti. Remains must be repatriated to the UK for cremation.
no_go_zones: Most of Port-au-Prince (gang-controlled areas)
Artibonite department
Cité Soleil and surrounding metropolitan zone
Anywhere outside Pétionville and secured international compound areas
risk_highlights: FCDO advises against all but essential travel
Gang violence controls large parts of Port-au-Prince and Artibonite
Civil registration has collapsed in gang-controlled areas
MSS (Kenyan-led multinational force) deploying since 2024 — security situation remains severe
Extremely limited mortuary capacity
Non-resident UK coverage from Kingston adds coordination time

nicaragua.british_representation: Non-resident. UK consular services provided by the British Embassy in San José, Costa Rica.
nicaragua.local_authority_involved: Registro del Estado Civil de las Personas (RECP), Ministerio del Interior
main_airports: Augusto C. Sandino International Airport (MGA), Managua
nicaragua.routing_notes: Repatriations route via Miami (American Airlines), Houston (United Airlines), or via other Central American hubs such as San José, Costa Rica (Copa Airlines via Panama). Miami connections to the UK are straightforward.
nicaragua.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
nicaragua.timeline_notes: Spanish civil law documentation through the Registro del Estado Civil de las Personas. Export permit from the Ministerio del Interior. Non-resident British Embassy San José coordinates consular registration. Documentation processes in Managua are generally predictable.
nicaragua.complexity_notes: Nicaragua's authoritarian government under President Daniel Ortega maintains strict controls on civil society and movement of foreign nationals. FCDO advises against non-essential travel. Political tensions following the 2018 crackdown on protests remain elevated. Bureaucratic processes involving foreign nationals can be unpredictable. Non-resident UK embassy coverage from San José adds coordination time.
required_documents: Consular death registration with British Embassy San José
nicaragua.religion_notes: Predominantly Catholic (c.58%), with a large Evangelical Protestant minority (c.33%). No significant Islamic burial timeline pressure.
nicaragua.cremation_notes: Cremation facilities are limited in Nicaragua. Repatriation of the body to the UK is the standard approach for UK nationals.
no_go_zones: Caribbean Coast Autonomous Regions (remote jungle areas)
Border areas with Honduras (Río Coco region)
risk_highlights: FCDO advises against all but essential travel
Authoritarian governance — bureaucratic unpredictability for foreign nationals
Non-resident UK coverage from San José adds coordination time
Remote Caribbean coast has limited logistics infrastructure

paraguay.british_representation: Non-resident. UK consular services provided by the British Embassy in Buenos Aires, Argentina.
paraguay.local_authority_involved: Dirección General del Registro Civil del Estado Civil de las Personas, Ministerio del Interior
paraguay.routing_notes: Repatriations route via Buenos Aires (Aerolíneas Argentinas or LATAM), São Paulo (LATAM/GOL), or via Miami. Buenos Aires Ezeiza is the most common connection point for UK routing.
paraguay.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
paraguay.timeline_notes: Spanish civil law documentation through the Dirección General del Registro Civil. Export permit from the Ministerio del Interior. Non-resident British Embassy Buenos Aires handles consular registration. Documentation in Asunción is generally efficient.
paraguay.complexity_notes: Paraguay has a functioning civil registration system and an established international airport at Asunción. The main complexity factors are non-resident UK diplomatic coverage from Buenos Aires, the Spanish/Guaraní language context, and the remote Chaco region in the west where infrastructure is sparse. No active conflict zones. The Tri-Border Area (Paraguay, Argentina, Brazil) around Ciudad del Este has elevated organised crime activity.
required_documents: Consular death registration with British Embassy Buenos Aires
paraguay.religion_notes: Predominantly Catholic (c.89%). No significant Islamic burial timeline pressure.
paraguay.cremation_notes: Cremation services are available in Asunción. If UK cremation is preferred, full repatriation is required. Ashes can also be repatriated; see the bringing ashes home guidance for Paraguay.
no_go_zones: Remote Chaco region (extreme west)
Tri-Border Area around Ciudad del Este (elevated organised crime)
risk_highlights: Non-resident UK coverage from Buenos Aires adds coordination time
Remote Chaco region has very limited infrastructure
Tri-Border Area (Ciudad del Este) has elevated organised crime

iraq.british_representation: British Embassy Baghdad (resident). British Consulate Erbil (resident, Kurdistan Region).
iraq.local_authority_involved: Civil Status Directorate (Mudiriyyat al-Ahwal al-Madaniyyah), Ministry of Interior, Medico-Legal Directorate
iraq.routing_notes: Repatriations from Baghdad typically route via Istanbul (Turkish Airlines), Dubai (Emirates/flydubai), or Amman (Royal Jordanian). Erbil has direct Turkish Airlines and other services. Basra uses Dubai as the primary hub.
iraq.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
iraq.timeline_notes: Iraqi civil registration through the Civil Status Directorate. The Medico-Legal Directorate handles forensic aspects where death is not from natural causes. Islamic burial pressure is significant — families should communicate urgency early. Security conditions vary sharply by region; timelines for Baghdad differ considerably from areas of active instability.
iraq.complexity_notes: FCDO advises against all travel to certain provinces and against all but essential travel to most of Iraq. Active security threats include militia groups, residual ISIL activity in disputed territories, and the risk of regional escalation. The Kurdistan Region (Erbil, Sulaymaniyah) is significantly more stable and has better-developed civil administration. Arabic and Kurdish documentation requirements. Islamic burial practices create timeline pressure. Non-Islamic funerals in some areas may face community or administrative resistance.
required_documents: Consular death registration with British Embassy Baghdad or Consulate Erbil
iraq.religion_notes: Predominantly Muslim (c.97% — approximately 64% Shia, 32% Sunni). Islamic burial within 24 hours is strongly observed. Repatriation to the UK for Muslim deceased may require specific documentation confirming Islamic rites were observed where possible. Christian and Yazidi minorities exist.
iraq.cremation_notes: Cremation is not practised in Iraq and facilities are not available. All remains must be repatriated in full.
no_go_zones: Anbar province
Nineveh Plains (disputed territory)
Areas near Syrian border
Southern Baghdad outskirts with militia presence
Areas of active ISIL activity in northern/western Iraq
risk_highlights: FCDO advises against all travel to some provinces
Active militia groups and residual ISIL in disputed areas
Risk of regional escalation involving Iran-backed groups
Islamic burial timeline pressure — 24-hour expectation
Significant documentation complexity in Arabic/Kurdish
Kurdistan Region (Erbil) substantially more manageable than Baghdad or southern Iraq

afghanistan.british_representation: No resident British Embassy. The UK Embassy in Kabul suspended operations in August 2021. UK nationals requiring consular assistance should contact the FCDO directly or the British Embassy in Islamabad, Pakistan.
afghanistan.local_authority_involved: Taliban-controlled civil administration, Ministry of Interior (Taliban government)
afghanistan.routing_notes: Limited commercial air access. Kabul is served by Flydubai, Ariana Afghan Airlines, and Kam Air with connections through Dubai, Istanbul, and Islamabad. Operational status of routes is subject to frequent change. Dubai is the primary hub for UK connections.
afghanistan.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
afghanistan.timeline_notes: The Taliban government that took power in August 2021 controls civil administration. Documentation processes are conducted through Taliban-controlled ministries. UK recognition issues mean consular registration must be handled via the British Embassy in Islamabad. Timelines are highly variable and unpredictable. Repatriation may not be achievable in all circumstances.
afghanistan.complexity_notes: FCDO advises against all travel to Afghanistan. The Taliban government has been in control since August 2021; the UK does not formally recognise the Taliban as the legitimate government of Afghanistan. This creates significant complications for consular engagement with Afghan authorities. Active conflict persists in some areas. The country has experienced decades of conflict and infrastructure is severely degraded outside Kabul. Islamic burial within 24 hours is observed — families must communicate urgency immediately. There are very few specialist repatriation firms with active Afghanistan capability.
required_documents: Death certificate (issued by Taliban-controlled civil authority)
Documentation from local authority confirming cause of death
Consular registration with British Embassy Islamabad
afghanistan.religion_notes: Almost entirely Muslim (Sunni majority, Shia minority including Hazara community). Islamic burial within 24 hours is strongly observed. Repatriation processes must be managed with awareness of this requirement.
afghanistan.cremation_notes: Cremation is not practised in Afghanistan. All remains must be repatriated in full.
no_go_zones: All areas outside Kabul and major provincial capitals
Kandahar province
Helmand province
Kunar and Nuristan provinces (eastern border)
Areas near Pakistan and Iranian borders
risk_highlights: FCDO advises against ALL travel to Afghanistan
No resident UK Embassy — British Embassy Islamabad provides limited assistance
Taliban control of civil administration creates UK-recognition complications
Active conflict in multiple provinces
Very limited specialist firm capability for active Afghanistan cases
Islamic burial timeline pressure — 24-hour expectation
Infrastructure severely degraded outside Kabul

syria.british_representation: No resident British Embassy. The British Embassy in Damascus closed in 2012. UK nationals should contact the FCDO directly. Limited consular assistance may be sought from the British Embassy in Beirut, Lebanon.
syria.local_authority_involved: Civil Status Directorate. Administration varies by region — post-December 2024 transition government in Damascus; Kurdish SDF in north-east; residual contested areas.
syria.routing_notes: Syria's aviation connectivity is severely limited. Damascus Airport resumed limited commercial operations post-December 2024 following the fall of Assad. Turkish Airlines and Middle Eastern carriers have begun resuming services cautiously. Most repatriation routing is via Beirut (Lebanon) overland then via Middle East hubs, or via Amman (Jordan).
syria.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
syria.timeline_notes: Syria is in political transition following the fall of Bashar al-Assad's government in December 2024. Civil administration is in the process of being reconstituted. Documentation processes are highly unpredictable. UK diplomatic presence is absent. Timelines are highly variable and some cases may not be achievable in the near term.
syria.complexity_notes: FCDO advises against all travel to Syria. The Assad government fell in December 2024 following a rapid advance by HTS (Hayat Tahrir al-Sham) and allied factions. Syria is in political transition. The north-east remains under Kurdish SDF administration. Former regime stronghold areas (Alawite coastal regions) have different local dynamics. Civil registration infrastructure is severely disrupted across much of the country. There is no UK Embassy in Syria. The British Embassy in Beirut provides very limited non-resident assistance subject to current capability. Repatriation is not achievable in many circumstances at present.
required_documents: Death certificate (from relevant local authority — varies by region)
Documentation from local authority on cause of death
Consular registration with British Embassy Beirut (subject to current capability)
syria.religion_notes: Predominantly Muslim (c.87% — Sunni majority, Alawite and other Shia minorities). Christian minority (c.10%). Islamic burial within 24 hours is observed in most communities.
syria.cremation_notes: Cremation is not practised in Syria and facilities are not available. All remains must be repatriated in full.
no_go_zones: All areas — FCDO advises against all travel
North-east (SDF-controlled)
Former conflict zones in Aleppo, Homs, Deir ez-Zor
Areas near Israeli and Lebanese borders
risk_highlights: FCDO advises against ALL travel to Syria
Political transition post-December 2024 — Assad government fell, HTS-led transition in Damascus
No UK Embassy since 2012 — very limited consular assistance available
Civil registration infrastructure severely disrupted
Multiple administrative zones with different authorities
Aviation connectivity severely limited
Repatriation may not be achievable in many current circumstances

yemen.british_representation: No resident British Embassy. The British Embassy in Sana'a closed in 2015. UK nationals should contact the FCDO directly. Limited consular assistance is provided by the British Embassy in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia.
yemen.local_authority_involved: Internationally recognised government (IRG) in Aden/Riyadh — Ministry of Interior; Houthi (Ansar Allah) administration in Sana'a and northern/western Yemen
main_airports: Aden International Airport (ADE) — main accessible gateway
Sana'a International Airport (SAH) — very limited, Houthi-controlled
yemen.routing_notes: Aden is the main accessible gateway under the internationally recognised government. Flydubai, Air Arabia, and Yemenia operate limited services via Dubai and Amman. Sana'a Airport has very limited and unpredictable scheduled service. Repatriation routing is extremely complex and subject to frequent change.
yemen.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
yemen.timeline_notes: Yemen has been in civil conflict since 2014-15. The country is effectively divided between the internationally recognised government (operating from Aden) and the Houthi movement (controlling Sana'a and much of northern/western Yemen). Documentation processes differ by region. Ceasefire arrangements have improved access in some areas. Timelines are highly unpredictable.
yemen.complexity_notes: FCDO advises against all travel to Yemen. Active conflict continues between the Houthis and the internationally recognised government, with Saudi-led coalition involvement. The 2022 UN-brokered truce has been extended but the underlying conflict is unresolved. Civil infrastructure is severely degraded. The humanitarian situation is among the world's worst. Repatriation from Houthi-controlled areas requires working through different authority channels than IRG-controlled areas. British nationals are extremely rare in Yemen; any such case should be treated as a specialist emergency.
required_documents: Death certificate (from relevant local authority — IRG or Houthi admin)
Consular registration with British Embassy Riyadh
yemen.religion_notes: Almost entirely Muslim (Sunni majority in south/east, Zaydi Shia majority in north). Islamic burial within 24 hours is strongly observed.
yemen.cremation_notes: Cremation is not practised in Yemen. All remains must be repatriated in full.
no_go_zones: All areas — FCDO advises against all travel
Active frontline areas (Sa'dah, Ma'rib, Taiz, Hudaydah governorates)
Red Sea coastal areas (Houthi missile/drone activity)
Houthi-controlled Sana'a and north-west
risk_highlights: FCDO advises against ALL travel to Yemen
Active civil conflict — Houthis vs IRG with Saudi-led coalition involvement
Country divided between two administrations (Houthi/IRG)
No UK Embassy since 2015 — very limited consular assistance
Aviation connectivity extremely limited
Humanitarian crisis — severely degraded infrastructure
Islamic burial timeline pressure — 24-hour expectation

turkmenistan.local_authority_involved: Civil Registry Office (ZAGS), Ministry of Internal Affairs
turkmenistan.routing_notes: Repatriations route via Istanbul (Turkish Airlines operates Ashgabat to Istanbul) or via Dubai (flydubai). Turkish Airlines is the most reliable carrier for UK-bound connections.
turkmenistan.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
turkmenistan.timeline_notes: Soviet-era civil registration system through ZAGS (Civil Registry Office). Ministry of Internal Affairs issues export permits. The British Embassy in Ashgabat is resident and provides consular support. The authoritarian governance environment means that bureaucratic processing can be unpredictable and may require specialist local contacts.
turkmenistan.complexity_notes: Turkmenistan is one of the world's most closed and authoritarian states. Foreign nationals have very limited freedom of movement. Internet and communications are heavily restricted, making coordination from the UK difficult. The FCDO advises that consular access to detained British nationals has historically been problematic. Civil registration follows the Soviet ZAGS model. Turkmen-language documentation adds a translation requirement. Access to remote desert regions (Karakum Desert, border areas with Afghanistan) is severely restricted.
required_documents: Consular death registration with British Embassy Ashgabat
