martinique.local_authority_involved: Mairie (municipal civil registry — mairie of the commune of death); Gendarmerie nationale or Police nationale (non-natural deaths)
martinique.main_airports: FDF (Martinique Aimé Césaire International Airport, Le Lamentin)
martinique.routing_notes: Martinique Aimé Césaire Airport (FDF) has direct Air France service to Paris Charles de Gaulle (CDG). Flight time approximately 9 hours. All UK repatriation routes via Paris CDG. No direct UK flights.
martinique.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
martinique.timeline_notes: Martinique is an overseas region and department (région et département d'outre-mer) of France — legally part of metropolitan France. French civil law applies in full. Death registration via the mairie of the commune where death occurred. Documentation in French. Certified English translation required for UK Coroner.
martinique.complexity_notes: French civil law — same legal system as metropolitan France. Non-resident British Embassy Paris. Documentation in French — certified English translation required. Direct Air France service to Paris CDG. Martinique has an established tourist industry with professional funeral services.
required_documents: Acte de décès (death certificate, mairie of commune of death)
Certified English translation of death certificate
Freedom from infection certificate (certificat de non-contagion)
Export permit (autorisation de sortie du territoire)
martinique.cremation_notes: Cremation is available in Martinique. Ashes may be repatriated to the UK in a sealed urn.
risk_highlights: Non-resident British Embassy Paris
All documentation in French — certified English translation required
No direct UK flights — routing via Paris CDG

guadeloupe.local_authority_involved: Mairie (municipal civil registry — mairie of the commune of death); Gendarmerie nationale or Police nationale (non-natural deaths)
guadeloupe.main_airports: PTP (Pointe-à-Pitre Les Abymes International Airport, Grande-Terre); outer island airports on Marie-Galante, Les Saintes, La Désirade
guadeloupe.routing_notes: Pointe-à-Pitre Les Abymes Airport (PTP) has direct Air France service to Paris Charles de Gaulle (CDG). Flight time approximately 8.5 hours. All UK repatriation routes via Paris CDG. Deaths on outer islands (Marie-Galante, Les Saintes, La Désirade) require inter-island transfer to Grande-Terre. Saint-Barthélemy and Saint-Martin (French) are separate collectivities — different jurisdictions.
guadeloupe.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
guadeloupe.timeline_notes: Guadeloupe is an overseas region and department of France — legally part of metropolitan France. French civil law in full. Death registration via the mairie of the commune of death. Documentation in French — certified English translation required for UK Coroner.
guadeloupe.complexity_notes: French civil law — same legal system as metropolitan France. Non-resident British Embassy Paris. Documentation in French — certified English translation required. Direct Air France to Paris CDG. Note: Saint-Barthélemy (St Barts) and Saint-Martin (French side) are separate collectivities — not part of Guadeloupe. Confirm correct jurisdiction.
required_documents: Acte de décès (death certificate, mairie of commune of death)
Certified English translation of death certificate
Freedom from infection certificate (certificat de non-contagion)
Export permit (autorisation de sortie du territoire)
guadeloupe.cremation_notes: Cremation is available in Guadeloupe. Ashes may be repatriated to the UK in a sealed urn.
risk_highlights: Non-resident British Embassy Paris
All documentation in French — certified English translation required
Outer islands (Marie-Galante, Les Saintes, La Désirade) require inter-island transfer
Saint-Barthélemy and Saint-Martin are separate jurisdictions — not Guadeloupe

reunion.local_authority_involved: Mairie (municipal civil registry — mairie of the commune of death); Gendarmerie nationale or Police nationale (non-natural deaths)
reunion.routing_notes: Roland Garros Airport (RUN) in Saint-Denis has direct Air France service to Paris Charles de Gaulle (CDG). Flight time approximately 11 hours. All UK repatriation routes via Paris CDG. No direct UK service.
reunion.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
reunion.timeline_notes: Réunion is an overseas region and department of France in the Indian Ocean — legally part of metropolitan France. French civil law in full. Death registration via the mairie. Documentation in French. Certified English translation required for UK Coroner. Réunion has a higher shark attack risk coastal zone; the western coast lagoon area is generally safer.
reunion.complexity_notes: French civil law — same legal system as metropolitan France. Non-resident British Embassy Paris. Documentation in French — certified English translation required. Indian Ocean location — somewhat further from UK than French Caribbean. Direct Air France Paris CDG service. Réunion has an active volcano (Piton de la Fournaise) in the south-east — check FCDO travel advice for current activity.
required_documents: Acte de décès (death certificate, mairie of commune of death)
Certified English translation of death certificate
Freedom from infection certificate (certificat de non-contagion)
Export permit (autorisation de sortie du territoire)
reunion.religion_notes: Predominantly Roman Catholic. Significant Hindu, Muslim, and Buddhist communities reflecting historical indentured labour migration.
reunion.cremation_notes: Cremation is available in Réunion. Ashes may be repatriated to the UK in a sealed urn.
reunion.no_go_zones: Active volcano zone (Piton de la Fournaise, south-east Réunion) — check FCDO and local prefectural advisories for current restricted areas.
risk_highlights: Non-resident British Embassy Paris
All documentation in French — certified English translation required
Active volcano (Piton de la Fournaise) — restricted zones possible in south-east
Shark attack risk on unprotected coastal beaches
Indian Ocean distance — routing via Paris CDG approximately 11 hours

new-caledonia.local_authority_involved: Service de l'État Civil (Civil Registration); Gendarmerie nationale or Police nationale (non-natural deaths). New Caledonia has a special status — a sui generis collectivity with its own government (Gouvernement de la Nouvelle-Calédonie).
new-caledonia.main_airports: NOU/GEA (La Tontouta International Airport, Nouméa); Magenta Airport (GEA) for domestic flights
new-caledonia.routing_notes: La Tontouta International Airport (NOU) near Nouméa has direct Air France and Air Calédonie International (Aircalin) service to Paris Charles de Gaulle (CDG). Also connections to Sydney (SYD) and Auckland (AKL). UK routing via Paris CDG is primary. Sydney routing provides an alternative via London connections.
new-caledonia.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
new-caledonia.timeline_notes: New Caledonia is a special collectivity (collectivité sui generis) of France in the South Pacific. French civil law applies, administered through its own government. Documentation in French. Remote Pacific location — approximately 18,900 km from UK. Political situation: New Caledonia held three independence referendums (2018, 2020, 2021) — all returned votes to remain part of France. Civil unrest has occurred; check FCDO advice for current situation.
new-caledonia.complexity_notes: Remote Pacific location — approximately 18,900 km from UK. Non-resident British Embassy Paris. Documentation in French — certified English translation required. New Caledonia has a complex political situation regarding independence, with periods of civil unrest. Outer islands (Loyalty Islands, Isle of Pines) require inter-island Air Calédonie transfer to Nouméa. Mining and industrial sector means some expat workers present.
required_documents: Acte de décès (death certificate, Service de l'État Civil)
Certified English translation of death certificate
Freedom from infection certificate (certificat de non-contagion)
Export permit (autorisation de sortie du territoire)
new-caledonia.religion_notes: Predominantly Roman Catholic. Significant Protestant minority (mainly Kanak community). Traditional Kanak spiritual practices.
new-caledonia.cremation_notes: Cremation is available in Nouméa. Ashes may be repatriated to the UK in a sealed urn.
risk_highlights: Remote Pacific location — approximately 18,900 km from UK
Non-resident British Embassy Paris
Political tension over independence — periods of civil unrest; check FCDO advice
Outer islands (Loyalty Islands, Isle of Pines) require inter-island transfer to Nouméa
All documentation in French — certified English translation required

gibraltar.british_representation: HM Government of Gibraltar (British Overseas Territory — no separate consulate required)
gibraltar.local_authority_involved: HM Coroner for Gibraltar; Royal Gibraltar Police
gibraltar.routing_notes: Direct British Airways and easyJet flights GIB–LGW. Short 2.5h flight. Land border with Spain at La Linea de la Concepcion.
gibraltar.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
gibraltar.timeline_notes: Short flight and English-speaking authorities typically make this one of the faster repatriations. Coroner involvement required for all deaths.
gibraltar.complexity_notes: British Overseas Territory, English common law, English-speaking authorities, direct short-haul flights. Straightforward process.
gibraltar.religion_notes: Gibraltar has Catholic majority plus Protestant and Jewish communities. Multiple faith funeral facilities available.
gibraltar.risk_highlights: None significant. Standard repatriation process applies.

falkland-islands.british_representation: HM Government of the Falkland Islands (British Overseas Territory — no separate consulate required)
falkland-islands.local_authority_involved: Falkland Islands Coroner; Royal Falkland Islands Police
falkland-islands.routing_notes: RAF charter flights Brize Norton (BZZ)–Mount Pleasant operated periodically. Commercial LATAM flights via Punta Arenas (PUQ) or Santiago (SCL) Chile. Very limited frequency — RAF routing most practical for repatriation.
falkland-islands.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
falkland-islands.timeline_notes: Remote South Atlantic location. RAF flight availability and Coroner process are primary scheduling factors. Delays possible.
falkland-islands.complexity_notes: Very remote British Overseas Territory with extremely limited flight options. RAF routing coordination adds logistical complexity. Allow significant time.
falkland-islands.religion_notes: Predominantly Christian (Protestant). Single main church in Stanley.
falkland-islands.cremation_notes: No cremation facility in the Falkland Islands.
falkland-islands.no_go_zones: Residual landmine fields from 1982 Falklands War remain in clearly marked areas. Do not enter marked minefields.
falkland-islands.risk_highlights: Residual landmines in marked areas (1982 conflict). Extreme weather and remote terrain. Very limited medical facilities outside Stanley.

saint-helena.country_name: Saint Helena, Ascension and Tristan da Cunha
saint-helena.british_representation: HM Government of Saint Helena (British Overseas Territory — no separate consulate required)
saint-helena.local_authority_involved: Saint Helena Coroner; Saint Helena Police Service
saint-helena.routing_notes: SA Airlink flights HLE–Johannesburg (JNB) approx twice weekly. Onward to UK via JNB. Ascension Island: RAF Wideawake Airfield — military access only. Tristan da Cunha: no airport — ship only (6-day voyage to Cape Town).
saint-helena.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
saint-helena.timeline_notes: Saint Helena Airport opened 2016 but flight frequency is very low. Tristan da Cunha is the most remote inhabited island on earth — ship-only access adds weeks.
saint-helena.complexity_notes: Extremely remote. Very limited flight frequency from Saint Helena. Ascension requires military clearance. Tristan da Cunha has no air access whatsoever.
required_documents: SA Airlink or RAF cargo documentation
saint-helena.religion_notes: Predominantly Christian (Anglican, Baptist, Catholic). Saint Helena has a small close-knit community.
saint-helena.cremation_notes: No cremation facility on Saint Helena, Ascension, or Tristan da Cunha.
saint-helena.risk_highlights: Extreme remoteness. Very limited medical facilities. Flight frequency from Saint Helena Airport is severely restricted. Tristan da Cunha has no air connection.

american-samoa.british_representation: British High Commission, Wellington, New Zealand (non-resident); Honorary British Consul, Apia, Samoa
american-samoa.local_authority_involved: American Samoa Department of Public Safety; Territory Medical Examiner; US federal and American Samoa territorial law applies
american-samoa.routing_notes: No direct UK flights. Routing via Honolulu (HNL) on Hawaiian Airlines then onward to UK from US gateway, or via Auckland (AKL) or Sydney (SYD). US transit documentation required.
american-samoa.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
american-samoa.timeline_notes: US legal requirements and limited flight connections are the main timing factors. US federal embalming and documentation rules apply for international air transport.
american-samoa.complexity_notes: US unincorporated territory — US federal law governs. Medical Examiner jurisdiction for non-natural deaths. Limited flight options and US transit documentation add complexity.
required_documents: US federal embalming and air transport certificate
american-samoa.religion_notes: Predominantly Christian (Congregationalist, Catholic, Methodist). Fa'asamoa (Samoan traditional customs) may influence family preferences.
american-samoa.cremation_notes: No cremation facility in American Samoa.
american-samoa.risk_highlights: Tropical cyclones June–November. Tsunami risk (Pacific Ring of Fire). Limited medical facilities outside Lyndon B. Johnson Tropical Medical Center in Pago Pago.

french-guiana.local_authority_involved: Mairie (civil registration); Gendarmerie nationale / Police nationale; French civil law — legally part of France
french-guiana.routing_notes: Direct Air France flights CAY–Paris CDG approx 9h. All documentation in French — certified English translation required.
french-guiana.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
french-guiana.timeline_notes: French overseas department — same process as mainland France. Direct flight makes routing straightforward. Translation and French civil registration are primary timing factors.
french-guiana.complexity_notes: Legally part of France. French civil law. All documentation in French requires certified translation. Security situation varies in interior jungle regions — Gendarmerie clearance required for non-natural deaths.
required_documents: Acte de deces (mairie) + certified English translation
french-guiana.religion_notes: Predominantly Catholic with Evangelical Christian minority. Indigenous and Maroon communities in the interior may have traditional practices.
french-guiana.no_go_zones: Interior jungle regions — limited infrastructure, significant journey times. Gold-mining areas near Suriname and Brazil borders have security risks.
french-guiana.risk_highlights: Malaria risk in interior jungle. Interior travel significantly lengthens repatriation. Gold-mining border areas have crime risk. Cayenne Kourou area (Ariane rocket launch site) has no special repatriation impact.

puerto-rico.british_representation: British Embassy, Washington DC (non-resident); Honorary British Consul, San Juan
puerto-rico.local_authority_involved: Puerto Rico Institute of Forensic Sciences (non-natural deaths); Puerto Rico Vital Statistics Registry; US federal and Puerto Rico territorial law applies
puerto-rico.routing_notes: Direct British Airways SJU–LHR approx 8.5h. Also American Airlines SJU–ORD/MIA/JFK then UK connections. US federal embalming and documentation rules apply for international air transport.
puerto-rico.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
puerto-rico.timeline_notes: US federal law governs body export from Puerto Rico — forensic clearance and US documentation requirements are the main timing factors. Direct BA flight to Heathrow is practical.
puerto-rico.complexity_notes: US unincorporated territory — US federal law and Puerto Rico territorial law apply. Institute of Forensic Sciences jurisdiction for non-natural deaths. US federal embalming certificate required. Direct UK flight available.
required_documents: Death certificate (Puerto Rico Vital Statistics Registry)
Institute of Forensic Sciences clearance (non-natural deaths)
US federal embalming and air transport certificate
puerto-rico.religion_notes: Predominantly Catholic with significant evangelical Protestant community. Full range of faith funeral services available in San Juan.
puerto-rico.risk_highlights: Hurricane season June–November. Occasional earthquake activity. Infrastructure in some rural inland areas may be affected by storm damage. Medical facilities in San Juan are good; rural areas have limited resources.

guam.local_authority_involved: Guam Medical Examiner; Guam Vital Statistics; US federal and Guam territorial law applies
main_airports: Antonio B. Won Pat International Airport (GUM)
guam.routing_notes: No direct UK flights. Routing via Tokyo Narita (NRT) or Tokyo Haneda (HND) on United Airlines or Japan Airlines, then UK connections from Tokyo. Also via Honolulu (HNL). Very long-haul transit — allow 24+ hours routing time.
guam.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
guam.timeline_notes: Remote Pacific location with no direct UK routing. US federal law requirements, Medical Examiner clearance, and multi-leg routing via Tokyo or Honolulu are the main timing factors.
guam.complexity_notes: US organised territory, western Pacific. US federal law governs body export. Medical Examiner jurisdiction for non-natural deaths. No direct UK routing — multi-leg via Tokyo or Honolulu. British Embassy Tokyo covers on non-resident basis.
required_documents: US federal embalming and air transport certificate
guam.religion_notes: Predominantly Catholic (Chamorro traditional faith). Also Protestant and non-denominational Christian communities.
guam.risk_highlights: Typhoon risk June–November. North Korea ballistic missile overflight risk (historically — check FCDO current advice). US military presence throughout the island. Medical facilities are reasonable for the Pacific.

us-virgin-islands.british_representation: British Embassy, Washington DC (non-resident); Honorary British Consul, Barbados covers Eastern Caribbean
us-virgin-islands.local_authority_involved: US Virgin Islands Medical Examiner; USVI Vital Statistics Bureau; US federal and USVI territorial law applies
main_airports: Cyril E. King Airport, St Thomas (STT)
Henry E. Rohlsen Airport, St Croix (STX)
us-virgin-islands.routing_notes: No direct UK flights. Routing via Miami (MIA) or San Juan Puerto Rico (SJU) then UK connections. American Airlines and United operate the main connections. US federal documentation requirements apply.
us-virgin-islands.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
us-virgin-islands.timeline_notes: Caribbean US territory — relatively close routing via Miami or San Juan. US federal law governs export. Medical Examiner clearance for non-natural deaths is the primary timing factor.
us-virgin-islands.complexity_notes: US organised territory. US federal law and USVI territorial law apply. Medical Examiner jurisdiction. Routing via Miami or Puerto Rico is well-established. St John and Water Island deaths require inter-island transfer to St Thomas or St Croix.
required_documents: US federal embalming and air transport certificate
us-virgin-islands.religion_notes: Predominantly Christian (Protestant, Catholic). Full range of services available on St Thomas and St Croix.
us-virgin-islands.cremation_notes: Cremation available on St Thomas and St Croix.
us-virgin-islands.risk_highlights: Hurricane season June–November. Crime in some areas of St Croix and St Thomas — check FCDO travel advice. Deaths on St John require transfer to St Thomas. Water Island: ferry transfer to St Thomas.

greenland.local_authority_involved: Police of Greenland (Politiet i Gronland); civil registration through Danish system; Danish law applies
greenland.routing_notes: Routing via Copenhagen (CPH) on Air Greenland or SAS. Kangerlussuaq is the main transit hub; Nuuk connections improving with new airport construction. Very limited frequency — Air Greenland schedule is the controlling factor.
greenland.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
greenland.timeline_notes: Limited Air Greenland flight frequency to Copenhagen is the primary scheduling constraint. Deaths outside Nuuk or Kangerlussuaq require inter-settlement transport which can take additional days.
greenland.complexity_notes: Autonomous territory of Denmark — Danish law applies. Very remote. Limited flight frequency from most settlements. Deaths outside main centres may require helicopter or small aircraft transfer. All documentation in Greenlandic/Danish — certified English translation required.
required_documents: Death certificate (Danish civil registration system) + certified English translation
Air Greenland or charter cargo documentation
greenland.religion_notes: Predominantly Evangelical Lutheran (Church of Greenland). Inuit traditional culture may influence family preferences in smaller communities.
greenland.cremation_notes: Cremation is not available in Greenland. Bodies are repatriated to Denmark or the UK for cremation.
greenland.no_go_zones: Arctic ice cap and remote inland icefield — limited emergency response. Travel outside settlements requires specialist preparation.
greenland.risk_highlights: Extreme Arctic conditions. Limited medical facilities outside Nuuk. Sea ice and weather can close airports and ports for days. Dog sledge routes and ice cap traverses carry serious risk. Very limited emergency response capacity in remote settlements.

faroe-islands.local_authority_involved: Faroese Police (Politiet i Faeroyerne); civil registration through Faroese system; Danish law applies with significant Faroese autonomy
faroe-islands.routing_notes: Atlantic Airways direct FAE–LGW (London Gatwick) approx 2.5h. Also Atlantic Airways FAE–CPH then Copenhagen–UK. Infrequent schedule — Atlantic Airways operates limited rotations.
faroe-islands.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
faroe-islands.timeline_notes: Atlantic Airways direct flight to Gatwick is very practical when available. Limited frequency is the main constraint — flights operate a handful of times per week.
faroe-islands.complexity_notes: Autonomous territory of Denmark. Danish law applies. English widely spoken. Direct London Gatwick flight available. Documentation in Faroese/Danish requires certified English translation but processes are straightforward.
required_documents: Death certificate (Faroese civil registration) + certified English translation
faroe-islands.religion_notes: Predominantly Evangelical Lutheran (Church of the Faroe Islands). Small Plymouth Brethren community.
faroe-islands.cremation_notes: No cremation facility in the Faroe Islands. Bodies are repatriated to Denmark or the UK for cremation.
faroe-islands.risk_highlights: Severe North Atlantic weather can delay flights significantly. Coastal cliff walking — significant fall risk on popular hiking routes. Very limited medical facilities outside Torshavn.

svalbard.local_authority_involved: Sysselmannen (Governor of Svalbard); Norwegian law applies; deaths investigated by Norwegian Coroner system
svalbard.routing_notes: SAS and Norwegian flights LYR–Oslo (OSL) then Oslo–UK. Also charter scientific/expedition flights. Only Longyearbyen has airport infrastructure; deaths elsewhere on the archipelago require snowmobile, boat, or helicopter transfer to Longyearbyen.
svalbard.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
svalbard.timeline_notes: Arctic location, extreme weather, and very limited infrastructure outside Longyearbyen are the primary timing factors. Deaths in the field (wilderness, glacier, research stations) require significant logistics to reach Longyearbyen.
svalbard.complexity_notes: High Arctic archipelago. Norwegian law but unique Svalbard Treaty status. Field deaths — glacier, wilderness, polar bear attack — are logistically complex. Governor (Sysselmannen) is the sole authority. All documentation in Norwegian requires certified English translation.
required_documents: Death certificate (Norwegian civil registration, Svalbard) + certified English translation
svalbard.religion_notes: Predominantly secular/Lutheran. Longyearbyen has a church. Russian Barentsburg settlement is Russian Orthodox.
svalbard.cremation_notes: No cremation facility in Svalbard. Bodies repatriated to mainland Norway or the UK for cremation.
svalbard.no_go_zones: Outside Longyearbyen, a firearm is legally required due to polar bear risk. The Norwegian government requires all visitors outside settlements to carry a rifle. Glaciers and sea ice carry serious crevasse and hypothermia risk.
svalbard.risk_highlights: Polar bear attacks outside settlements. Extreme cold, avalanche, and crevasse risk. Very limited medical facilities (Longyearbyen Hospital only). No infrastructure in the vast wilderness areas. Arctic storms can close the airport for days.

northern-mariana-islands.local_authority_involved: CNMI Medical Examiner; CNMI Vital Statistics; US federal and CNMI commonwealth law applies
northern-mariana-islands.routing_notes: No direct UK flights. Routing via Tokyo Narita (NRT) or Guam (GUM) then onward to UK. Limited international service — Japan Airlines and United are main carriers. US federal embalming and documentation requirements apply.
northern-mariana-islands.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
northern-mariana-islands.timeline_notes: Remote western Pacific location, US federal requirements, and limited flight frequency via Tokyo or Guam are the main timing factors. Deaths on outer islands (Tinian, Rota) require inter-island transfer to Saipan.
northern-mariana-islands.complexity_notes: US commonwealth territory, western Pacific. US federal law governs body export. CNMI Medical Examiner jurisdiction for non-natural deaths. No direct UK routing. British Embassy Tokyo covers non-resident. Deaths on Tinian or Rota require inter-island transfer.
required_documents: US federal embalming and air transport certificate
northern-mariana-islands.religion_notes: Predominantly Catholic (Chamorro and Carolinian traditional faith). Also non-denominational Protestant communities.
northern-mariana-islands.no_go_zones: WWII-era unexploded ordnance (UXO) reported in some areas — do not handle or disturb.
northern-mariana-islands.risk_highlights: Typhoon risk June–November. WWII unexploded ordnance in some locations. Limited medical facilities outside Saipan. Deaths on Tinian or Rota require inter-island transfer.

wallis-and-futuna.local_authority_involved: Administrator-Superior (Administrateur superieur); French civil law; Service de l'Etat Civil; Gendarmerie nationale
wallis-and-futuna.routing_notes: Air Caledonie International (Aircalin) flights WLS/FUT–Noumea (NOU) New Caledonia, then Air France/Aircalin NOU–Paris CDG. Very limited frequency — Aircalin schedule controls timing. Deaths on Futuna require inter-island flight to Wallis first.
wallis-and-futuna.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
wallis-and-futuna.timeline_notes: Extremely remote French Pacific collectivity. Very limited Aircalin flight frequency. Deaths on Futuna require transfer to Wallis first. Two-leg routing via Noumea to Paris before UK connection.
wallis-and-futuna.complexity_notes: One of the most remote inhabited French territories. French civil law. All documentation in French requires certified English translation. Two-leg routing via Noumea then Paris CDG. Very limited infrastructure and flight frequency.
required_documents: Acte de deces (Service de l'Etat Civil) + certified English translation
wallis-and-futuna.religion_notes: Almost entirely Roman Catholic. Traditional Polynesian customs (Tikanga) may influence family preferences.
wallis-and-futuna.cremation_notes: No cremation facility in Wallis and Futuna.
wallis-and-futuna.risk_highlights: Extremely limited medical facilities — one hospital on Wallis, basic clinic on Futuna. Cyclone risk. Deaths on Futuna may require emergency medevac to Wallis or New Caledonia for treatment. Very remote.

saint-pierre-and-miquelon.local_authority_involved: Sous-prefet; French civil law; mairie civil registration; Gendarmerie nationale
saint-pierre-and-miquelon.routing_notes: Air Saint-Pierre flights FSP to Halifax (YHZ) Nova Scotia Canada or St John's Newfoundland (YYT), then Air Canada/Air Transat to UK. Very limited frequency. Ferry to Fortune, Newfoundland is an alternative for body transfer in some circumstances.
saint-pierre-and-miquelon.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
saint-pierre-and-miquelon.timeline_notes: French overseas collectivity off the coast of Newfoundland. French civil law and documentation apply. Routing via Canadian airports then UK. Canadian transit requirements may apply.
saint-pierre-and-miquelon.complexity_notes: Small French collectivity 25km off the coast of Newfoundland, Canada. French civil law. All documentation in French requires certified English translation. Unusual routing via Canadian airports — Canadian transit documentation may apply.
required_documents: Acte de deces (mairie) + certified English translation
saint-pierre-and-miquelon.religion_notes: Predominantly Roman Catholic. Small, tight-knit community of approximately 6,000 people.
saint-pierre-and-miquelon.cremation_notes: No cremation facility in Saint Pierre and Miquelon.
saint-pierre-and-miquelon.risk_highlights: Dense fog and severe North Atlantic weather frequently disrupt air and ferry services. Very limited medical facilities — serious cases are evacuated to Halifax or St John's.

mayotte.local_authority_involved: Mairie (civil registration); Gendarmerie nationale / Police nationale; French civil law — overseas department of France since 2011
mayotte.routing_notes: Mayotte has no long-haul airport — aircraft are limited to narrowbody jets. Routing via Reunion (RUN) or Madagascar (TNR) then Air France to Paris CDG. Some Air Austral services. Two-leg routing adds transit time. No direct flights to mainland France.
mayotte.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
mayotte.timeline_notes: Mayotte has no direct mainland France connection. Two-leg routing via Reunion or Madagascar is required. French civil law documentation applies. Translation of French documents required for UK Coroner.
mayotte.complexity_notes: French overseas department in the Comoros archipelago, Indian Ocean. French civil law. No direct mainland France flight — routing via Reunion or Antananarivo Madagascar adds a transit leg. All documentation in French requires certified English translation.
required_documents: Acte de deces (mairie) + certified English translation
mayotte.religion_notes: Predominantly Muslim (Sunni, approximately 97%). Islamic burial traditions are strongly observed — timing pressure on repatriation is significant for Muslim families.
mayotte.cremation_notes: No cremation facility in Mayotte. Cremation is not practised by the predominantly Muslim population.
mayotte.risk_highlights: Civil unrest and migration crisis have affected Mayotte periodically — check FCDO current advice. Cyclone risk in the Indian Ocean. Limited medical facilities for complex cases. Inter-island boat travel between Grande-Terre and Petite-Terre carries some risk in poor weather.

pitcairn-islands.british_representation: Governor of the Pitcairn Islands (based in Auckland, New Zealand)
pitcairn-islands.local_authority_involved: Island Magistrate's Court; Governor of Pitcairn Islands
main_airports: None — no airport on Pitcairn. Supply vessel MV Claymore II from Mangareva (French Polynesia) every 3 months approximately. Mangareva reachable via Air Tahiti from Papeete (PPT).
pitcairn-islands.routing_notes: No airport exists on Pitcairn Island. Repatriation requires sea passage on the supply vessel from Pitcairn to Mangareva (French Polynesia), then Air Tahiti Mangareva–Papeete (PPT), then Air France PPT–CDG (~22h), then UK. Henderson, Ducie and Oeno Islands are uninhabited. Population approximately 45 residents. Vessel schedule is approximately quarterly; bespoke charter arrangements may be necessary.
pitcairn-islands.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
pitcairn-islands.timeline_notes: Highly variable. Dependent on supply vessel schedule (approximately quarterly). Bespoke charter may shorten timeline significantly but at exceptional cost.
pitcairn-islands.complexity_notes: Pitcairn is the world's most isolated permanently inhabited British territory. No airport, minimal infrastructure, quarterly supply vessels, and a population of approximately 45 residents create exceptional logistical difficulty. The Governor is based in Auckland. UK FCO involvement essential from the outset.
required_documents: Pitcairn Islands death certificate (issued by the Island Registrar)
UK consular death registration via Governor's Office
Cargo documentation for each transport leg
pitcairn-islands.religion_notes: Majority Seventh-day Adventist. Burial preferences should be discussed with the family and Island Pastor.
pitcairn-islands.cremation_notes: No cremation facility. Burial on Pitcairn or repatriation are the only options.
pitcairn-islands.no_go_zones: Henderson Island (UNESCO World Heritage Site, uninhabited), Ducie and Oeno atolls (uninhabited). No specific on-island no-go zones for Pitcairn village.
risk_highlights: Extreme isolation — supply vessel schedule governs all logistics
No airport — sea passage mandatory
Very small population means limited local funeral support
Governor based in Auckland — remote administration
French Polynesia transit documentation required (CITES not applicable but standard transit paperwork)

cocos-keeling-islands.british_representation: British High Commission Canberra, Australia (non-resident); Honorary Consul Perth
cocos-keeling-islands.local_authority_involved: Administrator of Cocos (Keeling) Islands; Australian Federal Police; Western Australia Coroner (for coronial matters)
main_airports: Cocos (Keeling) Islands Airport (CCK) — Cocos Keeling to Perth (PER) via Virgin Australia, approximately 3h
cocos-keeling-islands.routing_notes: Australian external territory administered under Australian law. Death registration and repatriation follow Australian (Western Australia) legal framework. Flights from CCK to Perth (PER) operate approximately twice weekly via Virgin Australia. Perth to UK: Qantas or partner airlines PER–LHR ~17h. UK consular assistance via BHC Canberra.
cocos-keeling-islands.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
cocos-keeling-islands.timeline_notes: Limited flight frequency from CCK to Perth can extend timeline. Once in Perth, standard international repatriation timelines apply.
cocos-keeling-islands.complexity_notes: Remote Australian external territory. Limited flight frequency, Australian law applies, and UK consular assistance operates through BHC Canberra. Two distinct island groups: Direction Island/West Island (airstrip) and Home Island (Cocos Malay community). Deaths on Home Island require transfer to West Island for repatriation.
required_documents: Australian export permit for human remains
cocos-keeling-islands.religion_notes: Home Island population is predominantly Muslim (Cocos Malay community). Islamic burial timing requirements may create urgency. West Island population is largely non-religious or Christian.
cocos-keeling-islands.cremation_notes: No cremation facility on the islands. Remains must be transferred to Perth or Australian mainland for cremation.
cocos-keeling-islands.no_go_zones: No formal no-go zones. North Keeling Island is a national park (Pulu Keeling) — uninhabited, restricted access.
risk_highlights: Limited flight frequency from CCK to Perth
Islamic community on Home Island — burial timing sensitivity
Australian law applies — UK families may be unfamiliar with process
Remote location — limited local funeral service infrastructure

christmas-island.british_representation: British High Commission Canberra, Australia (non-resident); Honorary Consul Perth
christmas-island.local_authority_involved: Administrator of Christmas Island; Australian Federal Police; Western Australia Coroner
main_airports: Christmas Island Airport (XCH) — Christmas Island to Perth (PER) via Virgin Australia, approximately 3.5h
christmas-island.routing_notes: Australian external territory in the Indian Ocean. Australian law applies. Flights from XCH to Perth (PER) operate approximately twice weekly via Virgin Australia. Perth to UK: Qantas or partner airlines PER–LHR ~17h. UK consular assistance via BHC Canberra.
christmas-island.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
christmas-island.timeline_notes: Limited flight frequency from XCH to Perth can extend timeline. Once in Perth, standard international repatriation timelines apply.
christmas-island.complexity_notes: Remote Australian external territory. Population approximately 2000, predominantly Chinese and Malay. Limited flight frequency and Australian legal framework. Asylum seeker processing centre on island adds administrative complexity. UK consular support operates remotely through BHC Canberra.
required_documents: Australian export permit for human remains
christmas-island.religion_notes: Significant Muslim (Malay) and Buddhist (Chinese) communities. Burial timing expectations may vary. Confirm religious observance needs with the family.
christmas-island.cremation_notes: No cremation facility on the island. Remains must be transferred to Perth for cremation.
christmas-island.no_go_zones: Immigration detention facility areas are restricted. The Flying Fish Cove and Settlement areas are the main population centres.
risk_highlights: Limited flight frequency from XCH to Perth
Diverse community — religious and cultural burial expectations vary
Asylum processing administration adds bureaucratic complexity
Australian law applies — UK families may be unfamiliar with process

norfolk-island.local_authority_involved: Administrator of Norfolk Island; Australian Federal Police; New South Wales Coroner (for coronial matters post-2016)
main_airports: Norfolk Island Airport (NLK) — direct flights to Sydney (SYD) ~2h and Brisbane (BNE) ~2.5h via Qantas/Air New Zealand
norfolk-island.routing_notes: Australian external territory since 2016 when self-government was abolished. Australian federal law applies. Direct flights from NLK to Sydney (SYD) or Brisbane (BNE) approximately 3x weekly. UK connections via SYD–LHR or BNE–LHR Qantas ~21h. UK consular assistance via BHC Canberra.
norfolk-island.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
norfolk-island.timeline_notes: Moderate complexity. Direct flights to Australia available approximately 3x weekly. Once in Sydney or Brisbane, onward connections to UK are straightforward.
norfolk-island.complexity_notes: Norfolk Island's governance changed in 2016 — Australian federal law now applies, replacing the previous self-governing framework. Families should be aware that the Coroner's jurisdiction now falls under New South Wales. Population approximately 1800. UK consular support via BHC Canberra.
required_documents: Australian death certificate (via Administrator's Office, Norfolk Island)
New South Wales Coroner's release (non-natural deaths)
Australian export permit for human remains
norfolk-island.religion_notes: Predominantly Christian. Anglican and Seventh-day Adventist communities present, reflecting the island's Bounty Mutiny heritage.
norfolk-island.cremation_notes: No cremation facility on Norfolk Island. Remains must be transferred to Sydney or Brisbane for cremation.
norfolk-island.no_go_zones: Kingston Archaeological Area (UNESCO World Heritage Site tentative list) has restricted access in some heritage zones.
risk_highlights: Flight frequency to Australia is limited (~3x weekly)
2016 governance change — Australian federal law applies, not self-governing rules
Remote South Pacific location — limited local funeral service infrastructure
UK consular assistance is remote via BHC Canberra

tokelau.british_representation: British High Commission Wellington, New Zealand (non-resident)
tokelau.local_authority_involved: Tokelau National Government (Taupulega); New Zealand Ministry of Foreign Affairs and Trade; New Zealand Coroner (in practice)
main_airports: No airport on any of the three atolls. Vessel from Apia, Samoa (APW) — approximately 2-day sea voyage. Apia served by Samoa Airways/Air New Zealand.
tokelau.routing_notes: Tokelau has no airport. The only access is by vessel from Apia, Samoa, taking approximately 2 days. All three atolls (Atafu, Nukunonu, Fakaofo) are accessible only by sea. Repatriation requires vessel transfer to Apia, then flights Apia (APW) to Auckland (AKL) ~3h, then Auckland–LHR Qantas/Air New Zealand ~26h. Vessel frequency is approximately fortnightly. New Zealand law and consular support applies throughout.
tokelau.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
tokelau.timeline_notes: Highly dependent on vessel schedule (approximately fortnightly). Sea voyage to Samoa adds 2 days minimum. Once in Auckland, standard international timelines apply.
tokelau.complexity_notes: Tokelau is a non-self-governing territory of New Zealand with no airport on any of its three atolls. All repatriation logistics depend on fortnightly vessel schedules. New Zealand law applies and the NZ Ministry of Foreign Affairs and Trade administers the territory. UK consular assistance via BHC Wellington. The combined sea-and-air journey to the UK requires careful coordination across multiple carriers and authorities.
required_documents: New Zealand death certificate (via Tokelau National Government and MFAT)
Airworthiness documentation for air transport from Samoa/Auckland
NZ export permit for human remains
tokelau.religion_notes: Predominantly Congregationalist Christian (Atafu and Fakaofo) and Roman Catholic (Nukunonu). Burial customs observed; discuss with family and local village elders.
tokelau.cremation_notes: No cremation facility. Burial on atoll or repatriation are the only options.
tokelau.no_go_zones: No formal no-go zones. All three atolls are low-lying — maximum elevation approximately 5 metres — creating acute climate vulnerability. No militarised or restricted zones.
risk_highlights: No airport — vessel schedule governs all logistics (fortnightly)
2-day sea voyage to Samoa before air repatriation can begin
Three atolls — death on a different atoll to the vessel stop adds further delay
New Zealand law applies — coordination via MFAT Wellington
Extreme remoteness with minimal local funeral infrastructure

akrotiri-and-dhekelia.british_representation: British High Commission Nicosia, Cyprus (sovereign UK territory — no consular representation needed; MOD/military chain of command)
akrotiri-and-dhekelia.local_authority_involved: Sovereign Base Area Administration (SBAA); SBA Police; Her Majesty's Coroner for the SBAs; Cyprus Republic authorities for civilian deaths off-base
main_airports: RAF Akrotiri (AKT) — military airfield; civilian connections via Larnaca (LCA) ~45 min or Paphos (PFO) ~30 min, both in Cyprus Republic
akrotiri-and-dhekelia.routing_notes: Akrotiri and Dhekelia are British Sovereign Base Areas in Cyprus, not part of the Republic of Cyprus. UK military personnel and dependants in the SBAs fall under MOD and SBAA jurisdiction. Civilian deaths on or near the base may involve both SBAA and Cyprus Republic authorities depending on location. Most repatriation uses civilian connections via Larnaca (LCA) to UK: Ryanair/Jet2/TUI direct to multiple UK airports approximately 4.5h. RAF Akrotiri may handle military repatriations via military air.
akrotiri-and-dhekelia.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
akrotiri-and-dhekelia.timeline_notes: Military chain of command handles service personnel repatriation. Civilian deaths follow Cyprus/SBAA procedures. Larnaca provides frequent direct UK connections, making civilian timelines relatively short once documentation is in order.
akrotiri-and-dhekelia.complexity_notes: The SBAs are UK sovereign territory, so standard FCDO consular assistance does not apply in the same way as overseas countries. Military families are supported through the MOD casualty notification chain. Civilians must navigate the SBAA Administration, which operates under a distinct legal framework from both UK domestic law and Cyprus Republic law.
required_documents: SBAA death certificate or Cyprus Republic death certificate (depending on location)
SBA Coroner's release (non-natural deaths) or Cyprus Coroner
akrotiri-and-dhekelia.religion_notes: Military population includes multiple denominations. The local Greek Cypriot population is predominantly Greek Orthodox. Confirm religious requirements with the family.
akrotiri-and-dhekelia.cremation_notes: No cremation facility in the SBAs. Nearest crematorium is in Cyprus Republic (Limassol). Transfer to Cyprus Republic or repatriation to UK for cremation.
akrotiri-and-dhekelia.no_go_zones: Active military zones within the SBAs are restricted. The UN Buffer Zone (Green Line) separates the Republic of Cyprus from Northern Cyprus and passes near some SBA boundaries.
risk_highlights: Distinct legal jurisdiction — neither UK domestic law nor Cyprus Republic law applies in full
Military families should engage MOD casualty team, not FCDO
Civilian deaths on base boundary may involve both SBAA and Cyprus Republic
RAF Akrotiri is a military airfield — civilian repatriation uses Larnaca or Paphos

isle-of-man.currency: Isle of Man Pound (IMP) / Pound Sterling (GBP)
isle-of-man.british_representation: Lieutenant Governor (UK Crown representative); no FCDO involvement — Isle of Man is a Crown dependency
isle-of-man.local_authority_involved: Isle of Man Coroner of Inquests; Isle of Man Constabulary; Isle of Man Register of Births, Deaths and Marriages
main_airports: Isle of Man Airport (IOM) — direct scheduled services to London Heathrow (LHR), Manchester (MAN), Liverpool (LPL), Edinburgh (EDI), Belfast (BHD) and Dublin (DUB). Flight time to London approximately 1h 10min.
isle-of-man.routing_notes: The Isle of Man is a Crown dependency of the British Crown, not part of the UK. It has its own parliament (Tynwald), legal system, and death registration procedures distinct from England and Wales. Repatriation to mainland UK uses direct scheduled flights from IOM to multiple UK airports. The process is closer to domestic transfer than international repatriation but requires Manx death registration and Coroner's procedures to be completed first.
isle-of-man.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
isle-of-man.timeline_notes: Good air connectivity to mainland UK. Timeline governed by Manx Coroner procedures for non-natural deaths. Straightforward natural deaths can be processed quickly.
isle-of-man.complexity_notes: The Isle of Man has its own legal system but shares close administrative ties with the UK. Death registration, Coroner procedures, and repatriation logistics are relatively straightforward. FCDO consular assistance does not apply — the Isle of Man is a Crown dependency, not a foreign country.
required_documents: Isle of Man death certificate (Registrar of Births, Deaths and Marriages)
Isle of Man Coroner's order (non-natural deaths)
isle-of-man.religion_notes: Predominantly Christian (Anglican, Methodist, Roman Catholic). No specific religious complexities in most cases.
isle-of-man.cremation_notes: Isle of Man Crematorium at Glencrutchery Road, Douglas. Cremation available on island before or instead of repatriation.
isle-of-man.no_go_zones: No restricted zones relevant to repatriation.
risk_highlights: Crown dependency — FCDO consular assistance does not apply
Own legal system — Manx death registration required before mainland transfer
TT motorcycle race period (late May/early June) increases visitor presence and accident risk

jersey.british_representation: Lieutenant Governor (UK Crown representative); no FCDO involvement — Jersey is a Crown dependency
jersey.local_authority_involved: Jersey Coroner; States of Jersey Police; Jersey Registry (deaths registration)
main_airports: Jersey Airport (JER) — direct scheduled services to London Heathrow (LHR), Gatwick (LGW), City (LCY), Stansted (STN), Bristol (BRS), Manchester (MAN), Edinburgh (EDI), Birmingham (BHX) and Exeter (EXT). Flight time to London approximately 1h.
jersey.routing_notes: Jersey is a Crown dependency of the British Crown, separate from the UK. It has its own legal system based on Norman customary law, distinct courts, and death registration procedures. Repatriation to mainland UK uses direct scheduled flights from Jersey Airport (JER) to multiple UK airports. The Jersey Coroner must authorise release of the body in non-natural deaths before repatriation proceeds.
jersey.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
jersey.timeline_notes: Excellent air connectivity to mainland UK. Jersey Coroner procedures are well-established. Timelines are short for natural deaths; non-natural deaths depend on Coroner enquiry.
jersey.complexity_notes: Jersey has well-developed repatriation infrastructure given its established links to the UK mainland. The main consideration is the Jersey legal system, which differs from English and Welsh law. FCDO consular assistance does not apply.
jersey.religion_notes: Predominantly Christian. No specific religious complexities in most cases. Jersey has a notable Portuguese community (Roman Catholic).
jersey.cremation_notes: Jersey Crematorium at Mont à l'Abbé, St Helier. Cremation available on island.
jersey.no_go_zones: No restricted zones relevant to repatriation.
risk_highlights: Crown dependency — FCDO consular assistance does not apply
Own legal system (Norman customary law) — Jersey Coroner and Registry must be engaged
High cost of living — some funeral services may reflect island pricing

guernsey.british_representation: Lieutenant Governor (UK Crown representative); no FCDO involvement — Guernsey is a Crown dependency
guernsey.local_authority_involved: HM Coroner of Guernsey; Guernsey Police; Greffe (civil registration office)
main_airports: Guernsey Airport (GCI) — direct scheduled services to London Gatwick (LGW), London City (LCY), Bristol (BRS), Manchester (MAN), Birmingham (BHX), Edinburgh (EDI) and Southampton (SOU). Flight time to London approximately 1h.
guernsey.routing_notes: Guernsey is a Crown dependency covering the island of Guernsey, plus the dependencies of Alderney, Sark, Herm and several smaller islets. Deaths on Alderney, Sark or Herm require transfer to Guernsey before air repatriation. Guernsey has its own legal system based on Norman customary law. HM Coroner of Guernsey handles non-natural deaths. Repatriation to mainland UK uses direct scheduled flights from GCI.
guernsey.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
guernsey.timeline_notes: Good air connectivity to UK mainland. Deaths on outer islands (Alderney, Sark, Herm) require sea or air transfer to Guernsey first, which can add 1-3 days.
guernsey.complexity_notes: Guernsey has well-established repatriation links to the UK mainland. The additional complication is the bailiwick's outer islands — Sark has no airport and Herm is accessible only by boat. Deaths on these islands require transfer to Guernsey. FCDO consular assistance does not apply.
required_documents: HM Coroner of Guernsey order (non-natural deaths)
guernsey.religion_notes: Predominantly Christian (Anglican, Roman Catholic, Methodist). No specific religious complexities in most cases.
guernsey.cremation_notes: Guernsey Crematorium at Foulon Cemetery, St Peter Port. Cremation available on island.
guernsey.no_go_zones: No restricted zones relevant to repatriation. Sark prohibits motor vehicles — access is by tractor, horse-drawn transport or boat.
risk_highlights: Crown dependency — FCDO consular assistance does not apply
Outer islands (Sark, Herm, Alderney) require transfer to Guernsey main island before air repatriation
Sark has no airport and no motor vehicles — logistics for deaths on Sark are more complex

bonaire.british_representation: British Embassy The Hague, Netherlands (non-resident); Honorary Consul Willemstad, Curaçao
bonaire.local_authority_involved: Public Entity Bonaire (Openbaar Lichaam Bonaire); Dutch Caribbean Police Force (KPCN); Court of First Instance Bonaire Sint Eustatius Saba (BES Islands); Civil Registry Bonaire
main_airports: Flamingo Airport (BON) — direct flights to Amsterdam Schiphol (AMS) ~9h via KLM; connections via Aruba (AUA) ~30 min or Curaçao (CUR) ~20 min for further routing
bonaire.routing_notes: Bonaire is a special municipality of the Netherlands (BES island — Bonaire, Sint Eustatius and Saba), meaning it is legally part of the Netherlands itself, not a separate country. Dutch law applies. Repatriation follows Netherlands legal procedures. KLM operates direct BON–AMS flights. UK consular assistance via British Embassy The Hague; Honorary Consul in Willemstad, Curaçao can provide closer support.
bonaire.typical_cost_notes: Contact specialist firm for current pricing
bonaire.timeline_notes: KLM direct flights BON–AMS operate several times weekly. Amsterdam to UK connections are straightforward. Dutch legal procedures for death registration and Coroner (Officier van Justitie) involvement in non-natural deaths govern the timeline.
bonaire.complexity_notes: Bonaire's status as a special municipality of the Netherlands (not a separate Caribbean country) means Dutch law applies directly. UK families should engage a specialist firm familiar with Dutch Caribbean legal procedures, which differ from Netherlands Antilles procedures that applied pre-2010. Sint Eustatius and Saba are the other BES islands — separate islands with the same framework.
required_documents: Dutch Coroner's release (Officier van Justitie, non-natural deaths)
Dutch export permit for human remains
bonaire.religion_notes: Predominantly Roman Catholic with Protestant and other Christian denominations. Papiamentu-speaking community has distinct cultural traditions around death and mourning.
bonaire.cremation_notes: No cremation facility on Bonaire. Remains must be transferred to Curaçao or the Netherlands for cremation.
bonaire.no_go_zones: Washington Slagbaai National Park covers the northern third of the island. No restrictions relevant to repatriation logistics.
risk_highlights: Dutch Caribbean legal framework — distinct from Netherlands Antilles pre-2010 rules
No cremation on island — transfer to Curaçao or Netherlands required
KLM direct service to Amsterdam — most straightforward routing
Honorary Consul in Willemstad (Curaçao) is closest UK consular support
